tasks.title: Implement Either pattern for domain error handling
tasks.description: Create the Either<L, R> type with Left and Right classes as the foundation for functional error handling across the domain layer
tasks.testStrategy:
Unit tests in `packages/core/test/shared/either.test.ts`:
- Test Left creation with `left()` helper
- Test Right creation with `right()` helper
- Test type narrowing: after `if (result.isLeft())` check, TypeScript should infer `result.value` as L type
- Test type narrowing: after `if (result.isRight())` check, TypeScript should infer `result.value` as R type
- Verify immutability of value property
- Test exhaustive checking with discriminated union pattern

subtasks.title: Create Either type with Left and Right classes
subtasks.description: Implement the core Either pattern with Left and Right classes including type guards in packages/core/src/shared/either.ts
subtasks.details:
Create `packages/core/src/shared/either.ts` with the complete Either implementation:

1. Implement `Left<L, R>` class:
   - Readonly `value: L` property
   - Constructor accepting `value: L`
   - `isLeft()` type guard returning `this is Left<L, R>`
   - `isRight()` type guard returning false

2. Implement `Right<L, R>` class:
   - Readonly `value: R` property
   - Constructor accepting `value: R`
   - `isLeft()` type guard returning false
   - `isRight()` type guard returning `this is Right<L, R>`

3. Define `Either<L, R>` type as discriminated union: `Left<L, R> | Right<L, R>`

4. Implement helper functions:
   - `left<L, R>(value: L): Either<L, R>` - creates Left instance
   - `right<L, R>(value: R): Either<L, R>` - creates Right instance

Ensure proper TypeScript type inference with discriminated unions. The type guards enable exhaustive pattern matching and type narrowing.

subtasks.title: Export Either types from shared module index
subtasks.description: Update packages/core/src/shared/index.ts to export all Either types and helper functions for consumption by other modules
subtasks.details:
Update `packages/core/src/shared/index.ts` to properly export the Either pattern:

```typescript
export { Either, Left, Right, left, right } from './either';
```

This makes the Either pattern available to:
- Other shared kernel modules
- Portfolio, Blog, and Contact bounded contexts
- Value Objects and Entities that need error handling

Verify exports are accessible by attempting to import from `@repo/core/shared` in TypeScript.
subtasks.testStrategy:
Verify exports with TypeScript compilation:
- Run `pnpm types` in packages/core
- Confirm no export errors
- Test import statement: `import { Either, left, right } from '@repo/core/shared'`

subtasks.title: Write comprehensive unit tests for Either pattern
subtasks.description: Create complete test suite covering type narrowing, helper functions, immutability, and all edge cases for the Either implementation
subtasks.details:
Create `packages/core/test/shared/either.test.ts` with comprehensive test coverage:

1. **Left creation and behavior:**
   - `left()` helper creates Left instance
   - `isLeft()` returns true
   - `isRight()` returns false
   - `value` property is accessible and correct

2. **Right creation and behavior:**
   - `right()` helper creates Right instance
   - `isLeft()` returns false
   - `isRight()` returns true
   - `value` property is accessible and correct

3. **Type narrowing:**
   - After `if (result.isLeft())`, TypeScript infers `result.value` as L type
   - After `if (result.isRight())`, TypeScript infers `result.value` as R type
   - Exhaustive pattern matching works correctly

4. **Immutability:**
   - `value` property is readonly
   - Cannot reassign value after construction

5. **Generic type inference:**
   - Type parameters are correctly inferred from arguments
   - Complex types (objects, arrays) work correctly

Use Vitest framework. Target 100% code coverage.
subtasks.testStrategy:
Run `pnpm test` in packages/core and verify:
- All tests pass
- 100% code coverage for either.ts
- Type narrowing works in test code (no TypeScript errors)
- Tests follow naming pattern: 'should <expected behavior> when <context>'

tasks.title: Migrate all Value Objects to Either pattern
tasks.description: Refactor all VOs (Id, DateTime, Text, Name, Url, EmploymentType, Fluency, LocationType, SkillType, LocalizedText) from throw-based to Either-based error handling
tasks.details:
For each VO in `packages/core/src/shared/vo/`:

1. Change static factory method to return `Either<ValidationError, T>`:
```typescript
static create(value: string): Either<ValidationError, VoName> {
  const validation = Validator.new(value)
    .required('Value is required')
    .min(3, 'Minimum 3 characters')
    .validate();
  
  if (!validation.isValid && validation.error) {
    return left(new ValidationError({ 
      code: 'INVALID_VO_NAME',
      message: validation.error 
    }));
  }
  
  return right(new VoName(value));
}
```

2. For Id VO:
   - Create `Id.generate()` that returns `Id` directly (no Either) for generating new UUIDs
   - Keep `Id.create(value)` returning `Either<ValidationError, Id>` for validation

3. Remove all `throw` statements for domain errors

4. Standardize error codes to `INVALID_<NAME>` format (e.g., `INVALID_ID`, `INVALID_TEXT`)

5. Translate all Portuguese error messages to English

6. Delete `packages/core/src/shared/i18n/ERROR_MESSAGE.ts` (no longer needed)

7. Update exports in `packages/core/src/shared/vo/index.ts`

VOs to migrate:
- Id
- DateTime
- Text
- Name
- Url
- EmploymentType
- Fluency
- LocationType
- SkillType
- LocalizedText

- Rewrite tests to use Either pattern (no `.toThrow()` assertions)

- Test each validation rule: `expect(result.isLeft()).toBe(true)` and verify error code

subtasks.title: Implement Slug Value Object with kebab-case validation
subtasks.description: Create the Slug VO in packages/core/src/shared/vo/Slug.ts with regex-based validation for kebab-case format, minimum length checks, and toPath() method
subtasks.details:
Implement Slug.ts extending ValueObject<string> with:
- Private static SLUG_REGEX = /^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$/ for kebab-case validation
- Private constructor taking normalized string value
- Static create(raw: string) method with Either<ValidationError, Slug> return:
  - Validate min 3 characters (return Left with INVALID_SLUG if violated)
  - Normalize to lowercase and trim
  - Test against SLUG_REGEX (return Left with INVALID_SLUG if fails)
  - Return Right(new Slug(normalized)) on success
- Public toPath(): string method returning `/${this.value}`
- Import Either, left, right from '../either'
- Import ValidationError from '../errors'
- Import ValueObject from '../base/ValueObject'

subtasks.title: Implement Image Value Object composing Url and LocalizedText
subtasks.description: Create the Image VO in packages/core/src/shared/vo/Image.ts as a composite value object that combines Url and LocalizedText with proper Either chaining
subtasks.details:
Implement Image.ts extending ValueObject<{ url: Url; alt: LocalizedText }> with:
- Private constructor taking url: Url and alt: LocalizedText parameters
- Static create(urlStr: string, alt: Record<string, string>) method:
  - Call Url.create(urlStr) and check if Left, return early with error
  - Call LocalizedText.create(alt) and check if Left, return early with error
  - Return Right(new Image(urlResult.value, altResult.value)) on success
- Getter methods: get url(): Url and get alt(): LocalizedText
- Properly handle Either composition from multiple VOs
- Import Url and LocalizedText from their respective files
- Import Either, left, right from '../either'
- Import ValidationError from '../errors'
- Import ValueObject from '../base/ValueObject'

subtasks.title: Implement DateRange Value Object with start/end validation
subtasks.description: Create the DateRange VO in packages/core/src/shared/vo/DateRange.ts with DateTime composition, start-before-end validation, and isActive() business logic
subtasks.details:
Implement DateRange.ts extending ValueObject<{ startAt: DateTime; endAt?: DateTime }> with:
- Private constructor taking startAt: DateTime and optional endAt?: DateTime
- Static create(start: string, end?: string) method:
  - Call DateTime.create(start), return Left if invalid
  - If end provided, call DateTime.create(end), return Left if invalid
  - Validate startAt.ms <= endAt.ms, return Left with INVALID_DATE_RANGE if violated
  - Return Right(new DateRange(startResult.value, endAt)) on success
- Public isActive(): boolean method returning true if endAt is undefined
- Getter methods: get startAt(): DateTime and get endAt(): DateTime | undefined
- Import DateTime from './DateTime'
- Import Either, left, right from '../either'
- Import ValidationError from '../errors'
- Import ValueObject from '../base/ValueObject'

subtasks.title: Create comprehensive unit tests for Slug VO
subtasks.description: Write unit tests in packages/core/test/shared/vo/Slug.test.ts covering all validation rules, edge cases, and the toPath() method
subtasks.details:
Create Slug.test.ts with describe block testing:
- Valid kebab-case slugs ('my-slug', 'slug-123', 'abc') return Right
- Invalid formats return Left with INVALID_SLUG:
  - Uppercase letters ('My-Slug')
  - Spaces ('my slug')
  - Special characters ('my_slug', 'my.slug', 'my@slug')
  - Leading/trailing hyphens ('-slug', 'slug-')
  - Consecutive hyphens ('my--slug')
- Min length validation:
  - Strings < 3 chars ('ab', 'a') return Left
  - Empty string and whitespace-only return Left
- toPath() method returns '/slug-name' format
- Edge cases: trim whitespace before validation
- Use Vitest (describe, it, expect) with proper assertions
- Test both isLeft() and isRight() type guards
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests with Vitest covering all validation rules, edge cases, and toPath() method. Verify Left returns contain proper ValidationError with INVALID_SLUG code.

subtasks.title: Create comprehensive unit tests for Image VO
subtasks.description: Write unit tests in packages/core/test/shared/vo/Image.test.ts covering composition of Url and LocalizedText with proper error handling
subtasks.details:
Create Image.test.ts with describe block testing:
- Valid composition: valid URL + valid LocalizedText returns Right
- Invalid URL composition:
  - Invalid URL string returns Left with Url's validation error
  - Verify error is propagated from Url.create()
- Invalid LocalizedText composition:
  - Invalid localized text returns Left with LocalizedText's validation error
  - Empty translations, missing required locales
- Getter methods:
  - image.url returns Url instance
  - image.alt returns LocalizedText instance
- Either chaining: verify both VOs are validated in sequence
- Test with mock data for URLs and localized alt text
- Use Vitest (describe, it, expect) with proper assertions
- Verify Left returns contain appropriate ValidationError from composed VOs
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests with Vitest covering composition scenarios, error propagation from Url and LocalizedText, and getter methods. Test Either chaining with valid/invalid inputs.

subtasks.title: Create comprehensive unit tests for DateRange VO and test data providers
subtasks.description: Write unit tests in packages/core/test/shared/vo/DateRange.test.ts covering start/end validation and business logic, plus create test data providers for all three VOs
subtasks.details:
Create DateRange.test.ts with describe block testing:
- Valid range: start <= end returns Right
- Invalid range: start > end returns Left with INVALID_DATE_RANGE
- Optional end date: omitting end date returns Right
- isActive() method:
  - Returns true when endAt is undefined (ongoing period)
  - Returns false when endAt is defined (completed period)
- Getter methods: startAt and endAt return correct DateTime instances
- Edge cases: same start and end date (valid), invalid DateTime strings

Create test data providers in packages/core/test/data/bases/:
- SlugData.ts: valid slugs ('my-project', 'test-123'), invalid slugs ('My-Project', 'test_case')
- ImageData.ts: valid Image objects with URLs and localized alt text
- DateRangeData.ts: valid ranges, invalid ranges, active/inactive periods

Use Vitest with proper assertions for all tests
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests with Vitest covering validation rules, boundary conditions, isActive() business logic, and getter methods. Create reusable test data providers for all three VOs to support testing across the codebase.

subtasks.title: Update SkillFactory.bulk() to return Either with indexed error messages
subtasks.description: Refactor SkillFactory.bulk() method from returning Skill[] to returning Either<DomainError, Skill[]>, iterate over skill props array, and include index in error messages for failed validations
subtasks.details:
1. Locate SkillFactory.bulk() method (currently at line 4-6)
2. Change return type from Skill[] to Either<DomainError, Skill[]>
3. Initialize empty array: const skills: Skill[] = []
4. Loop with index: for (let i = 0; i < skillsProps.length; i++)
5. Call Skill.create(skillsProps[i]) for each item
6. Check if result.isLeft(): return left(new ValidationError({ code: 'INVALID_SKILL_AT_INDEX', message: `Invalid skill at index ${i}: ${result.value.message}`, details: { index: i, error: result.value } }))
7. If result.isRight(): skills.push(result.value)
8. After loop completes: return right(skills)
9. Update any callers of bulk() to handle Either return type
subtasks.testStrategy: Test SkillFactory.bulk() with all valid skills returns Right<Skill[]>. Test SkillFactory.bulk() with one invalid skill at index 2 returns Left with error message including 'index 2'. Test SkillFactory.bulk() with empty array returns Right<[]>. Test SkillFactory.bulk() with invalid skill at index 0 returns Left with index 0 in error.

subtasks.details: In Profile.create() static method, add first validation check: if (props.featuredProjectSlugs.length > this.MAX_FEATURED_PROJECTS) return left(new ValidationError({ code: 'TOO_MANY_FEATURED_PROJECTS', message: `Maximum ${this.MAX_FEATURED_PROJECTS} featured projects allowed, got ${props.featuredProjectSlugs.length}` })). Place this check at the beginning of create() method before any VO validations. This enforces the core business invariant that Profile aggregate must protect. Import ValidationError from shared/errors and Either/left/right from shared/either.

tasks.title: Create packages/core architecture documentation
tasks.description: Document bounded contexts, architectural decisions, layer rules, and ubiquitous language for the domain layer
tasks.details:
Create comprehensive documentation for packages/core:

1. `packages/core/ARCHITECTURE.md`:
   - Bounded context map (Portfolio, Blog, Contact, Shared Kernel)
   - Dependency rules between contexts
   - Layer isolation rules (what core can/cannot import)
   - Visual diagram of context boundaries
   - Export structure (@repo/core/portfolio, @repo/core/shared, etc.)

2. `packages/core/GLOSSARY.md`:
   - Ubiquitous Language definitions for Portfolio context:
     - Project, Experience, Profile, Skill
     - Value Objects: Slug, Image, DateRange, LocalizedText, etc.
   - Term definitions with examples
   - Relationships between domain concepts

3. `packages/core/decisions/adr-template.md`:
```markdown
# ADR-XXX: [Title]

## Status
[Proposed | Accepted | Deprecated | Superseded]

## Context
[What is the issue we're facing?]

## Decision
[What did we decide?]

## Consequences
[What becomes easier or harder?]

## Alternatives Considered
[What other options did we evaluate?]
```

4. `packages/core/decisions/ADR-001-repository-interfaces-in-core.md`:
```markdown
# ADR-001: Repository Interfaces Belong in Core, Not Application

## Status
Accepted

## Context
In Clean Architecture, repository interfaces (ports) can be placed in either the domain layer (core) or application layer. We need to decide where they belong in our monorepo.

## Decision
Repository interfaces are defined in `packages/core` alongside their respective entities, not in `packages/application`.

Structure:
- `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/project/repositories/IProjectRepository.ts`
- `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/experience/repositories/IExperienceRepository.ts`

## Consequences

**Positive:**
- Interfaces live with their aggregate roots (high cohesion)
- Domain defines its own persistence contract
- Application layer doesn't need to define infrastructure contracts
- Easier to understand what operations are available per entity

**Negative:**
- Some Clean Architecture implementations put interfaces in application
- Requires clear documentation of this architectural choice

## Alternatives Considered

1. **Application Layer Interfaces**: Place in `packages/application/ports/repositories/`
   - Rejected: Separates interface from entity, lower cohesion

2. **Separate Ports Package**: Create `packages/ports`
   - Rejected: Over-engineering for monorepo, adds unnecessary complexity
```

Ensure documentation is referenced from root `CLAUDE.md`.
tasks.testStrategy:
Documentation validation:

1. Completeness:
   - ARCHITECTURE.md covers all bounded contexts
   - GLOSSARY.md defines all domain terms
   - ADR-001 follows template structure

2. Accuracy:
   - Visual diagrams match actual code structure
   - Export paths match package.json exports
   - Glossary definitions align with code

3. Formatting:
   - All markdown files render correctly
   - Links between documents work
   - Code blocks have proper syntax highlighting

4. Integration:
   - Reference added to root CLAUDE.md
   - Documentation discoverable by developers

5. Review:
   - Technical accuracy review by team
   - No contradictions with CLAUDE.md guidelines

subtasks.title: Write ADR-001 for repository interface placement decision
subtasks.description: Document architectural decision to place repository interfaces in core layer alongside entities rather than in application layer
subtasks.details:
Create `packages/core/decisions/ADR-001-repository-interfaces-in-core.md`:

**Status**: Accepted

**Context**: Explain that Clean Architecture allows repository interfaces (ports) in either domain or application layer. We needed to decide placement for monorepo.

**Decision**: Repository interfaces defined in `packages/core` alongside entities:
- `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/project/repositories/IProjectRepository.ts`
- `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/experience/repositories/IExperienceRepository.ts`
- Pattern: interfaces live with their aggregate roots

**Consequences**:
- **Positive**: High cohesion (interface + entity together), domain defines persistence contract, easier discoverability
- **Negative**: Differs from some Clean Architecture implementations, requires documentation

**Alternatives Considered**:
1. **Application Layer Interfaces** (`packages/application/ports/repositories/`): Rejected due to lower cohesion
2. **Separate Ports Package** (`packages/ports`): Rejected as over-engineering for monorepo

Reference actual repository interfaces from packages/core/src/portfolio.
subtasks.testStrategy:
ADR-001 validation:

tasks.title: Implement Either pattern for domain error handling

- Test type narrowing: after `if (result.isRight())` check, TypeScript should infer `result.value` as R type
- Verify immutability of value property
- Test exhaustive checking with discriminated union pattern

subtasks.title: Create Either type with Left and Right classes
subtasks.description: Implement the core Either pattern with Left and Right classes including type guards in packages/core/src/shared/either.ts
subtasks.details:
Create `packages/core/src/shared/either.ts` with the complete Either implementation:

1. Implement `Left<L, R>` class:
   - Readonly `value: L` property
   - Constructor accepting `value: L`
   - `isLeft()` type guard returning `this is Left<L, R>`
   - `isRight()` type guard returning false

2. Implement `Right<L, R>` class:
   - Readonly `value: R` property
   - Constructor accepting `value: R`
   - `isLeft()` type guard returning false
   - `isRight()` type guard returning `this is Right<L, R>`

3. Define `Either<L, R>` type as discriminated union: `Left<L, R> | Right<L, R>`

4. Implement helper functions:
   - `left<L, R>(value: L): Either<L, R>` - creates Left instance
   - `right<L, R>(value: R): Either<L, R>` - creates Right instance

Ensure proper TypeScript type inference with discriminated unions. The type guards enable exhaustive pattern matching and type narrowing.


subtasks.title: Write comprehensive unit tests for Either pattern
subtasks.description: Create complete test suite covering type narrowing, helper functions, immutability, and all edge cases for the Either implementation
subtasks.details:
Create `packages/core/test/shared/either.test.ts` with comprehensive test coverage:

1. **Left creation and behavior:**
   - `left()` helper creates Left instance
   - `isLeft()` returns true
   - `isRight()` returns false
   - `value` property is accessible and correct

2. **Right creation and behavior:**
   - `right()` helper creates Right instance
   - `isLeft()` returns false
   - `isRight()` returns true
   - `value` property is accessible and correct

3. **Type narrowing:**
   - After `if (result.isLeft())`, TypeScript infers `result.value` as L type
   - After `if (result.isRight())`, TypeScript infers `result.value` as R type
   - Exhaustive pattern matching works correctly

4. **Immutability:**
   - `value` property is readonly
   - Cannot reassign value after construction

5. **Generic type inference:**
   - Type parameters are correctly inferred from arguments
   - Complex types (objects, arrays) work correctly

Use Vitest framework. Target 100% code coverage.
subtasks.testStrategy:
Run `pnpm test` in packages/core and verify:
- All tests pass
- 100% code coverage for either.ts
- Type narrowing works in test code (no TypeScript errors)
- Tests follow naming pattern: 'should <expected behavior> when <context>'

tasks.title: Migrate all Value Objects to Either pattern
tasks.description: Refactor all VOs (Id, DateTime, Text, Name, Url, EmploymentType, Fluency, LocationType, SkillType, LocalizedText) from throw-based to Either-based error handling
tasks.details:
For each VO in `packages/core/src/shared/vo/`:

1. Change static factory method to return `Either<ValidationError, T>`:
```typescript
static create(value: string): Either<ValidationError, VoName> {
  const validation = Validator.new(value)
    .required('Value is required')
    .min(3, 'Minimum 3 characters')
    .validate();
  
  if (!validation.isValid && validation.error) {
    return left(new ValidationError({ 
      code: 'INVALID_VO_NAME',
      message: validation.error 
    }));
  }
  
  return right(new VoName(value));
}
```

2. For Id VO:
   - Create `Id.generate()` that returns `Id` directly (no Either) for generating new UUIDs
   - Keep `Id.create(value)` returning `Either<ValidationError, Id>` for validation

3. Remove all `throw` statements for domain errors

4. Standardize error codes to `INVALID_<NAME>` format (e.g., `INVALID_ID`, `INVALID_TEXT`)

5. Translate all Portuguese error messages to English

6. Delete `packages/core/src/shared/i18n/ERROR_MESSAGE.ts` (no longer needed)

7. Update exports in `packages/core/src/shared/vo/index.ts`

VOs to migrate:
- Id
- DateTime
- Text
- Name
- Url
- EmploymentType
- Fluency
- LocationType
- SkillType
- LocalizedText

subtasks.title: Implement Slug Value Object with kebab-case validation
subtasks.description: Create the Slug VO in packages/core/src/shared/vo/Slug.ts with regex-based validation for kebab-case format, minimum length checks, and toPath() method
subtasks.details:
Implement Slug.ts extending ValueObject<string> with:
- Private static SLUG_REGEX = /^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$/ for kebab-case validation
- Private constructor taking normalized string value
- Static create(raw: string) method with Either<ValidationError, Slug> return:
  - Validate min 3 characters (return Left with INVALID_SLUG if violated)
  - Normalize to lowercase and trim
  - Test against SLUG_REGEX (return Left with INVALID_SLUG if fails)
  - Return Right(new Slug(normalized)) on success
- Public toPath(): string method returning `/${this.value}`
- Import Either, left, right from '../either'
- Import ValidationError from '../errors'
- Import ValueObject from '../base/ValueObject'

subtasks.title: Implement Image Value Object composing Url and LocalizedText
subtasks.description: Create the Image VO in packages/core/src/shared/vo/Image.ts as a composite value object that combines Url and LocalizedText with proper Either chaining
subtasks.details:
Implement Image.ts extending ValueObject<{ url: Url; alt: LocalizedText }> with:
- Private constructor taking url: Url and alt: LocalizedText parameters
- Static create(urlStr: string, alt: Record<string, string>) method:
  - Call Url.create(urlStr) and check if Left, return early with error
  - Call LocalizedText.create(alt) and check if Left, return early with error
  - Return Right(new Image(urlResult.value, altResult.value)) on success
- Getter methods: get url(): Url and get alt(): LocalizedText
- Properly handle Either composition from multiple VOs
- Import Url and LocalizedText from their respective files
- Import Either, left, right from '../either'
- Import ValidationError from '../errors'
- Import ValueObject from '../base/ValueObject'

subtasks.title: Implement DateRange Value Object with start/end validation
subtasks.description: Create the DateRange VO in packages/core/src/shared/vo/DateRange.ts with DateTime composition, start-before-end validation, and isActive() business logic
subtasks.details:
Implement DateRange.ts extending ValueObject<{ startAt: DateTime; endAt?: DateTime }> with:
- Private constructor taking startAt: DateTime and optional endAt?: DateTime
- Static create(start: string, end?: string) method:
  - Call DateTime.create(start), return Left if invalid
  - If end provided, call DateTime.create(end), return Left if invalid
  - Validate startAt.ms <= endAt.ms, return Left with INVALID_DATE_RANGE if violated
  - Return Right(new DateRange(startResult.value, endAt)) on success
- Public isActive(): boolean method returning true if endAt is undefined
- Getter methods: get startAt(): DateTime and get endAt(): DateTime | undefined
- Import DateTime from './DateTime'
- Import Either, left, right from '../either'
- Import ValidationError from '../errors'
- Import ValueObject from '../base/ValueObject'

subtasks.title: Create comprehensive unit tests for Slug VO
subtasks.description: Write unit tests in packages/core/test/shared/vo/Slug.test.ts covering all validation rules, edge cases, and the toPath() method
subtasks.details:
Create Slug.test.ts with describe block testing:
- Valid kebab-case slugs ('my-slug', 'slug-123', 'abc') return Right
- Invalid formats return Left with INVALID_SLUG:
  - Uppercase letters ('My-Slug')
  - Spaces ('my slug')
  - Special characters ('my_slug', 'my.slug', 'my@slug')
  - Leading/trailing hyphens ('-slug', 'slug-')
  - Consecutive hyphens ('my--slug')
- Min length validation:
  - Strings < 3 chars ('ab', 'a') return Left
  - Empty string and whitespace-only return Left
- toPath() method returns '/slug-name' format
- Edge cases: trim whitespace before validation
- Use Vitest (describe, it, expect) with proper assertions
- Test both isLeft() and isRight() type guards
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests with Vitest covering all validation rules, edge cases, and toPath() method. Verify Left returns contain proper ValidationError with INVALID_SLUG code.

subtasks.title: Update SkillFactory.bulk() to return Either with indexed error messages
subtasks.description: Refactor SkillFactory.bulk() method from returning Skill[] to returning Either<DomainError, Skill[]>, iterate over skill props array, and include index in error messages for failed validations
subtasks.details:
1. Locate SkillFactory.bulk() method (currently at line 4-6)
2. Change return type from Skill[] to Either<DomainError, Skill[]>
3. Initialize empty array: const skills: Skill[] = []
4. Loop with index: for (let i = 0; i < skillsProps.length; i++)
5. Call Skill.create(skillsProps[i]) for each item
6. Check if result.isLeft(): return left(new ValidationError({ code: 'INVALID_SKILL_AT_INDEX', message: `Invalid skill at index ${i}: ${result.value.message}`, details: { index: i, error: result.value } }))
7. If result.isRight(): skills.push(result.value)
8. After loop completes: return right(skills)
9. Update any callers of bulk() to handle Either return type

tasks.title: Create packages/core architecture documentation
tasks.description: Document bounded contexts, architectural decisions, layer rules, and ubiquitous language for the domain layer
tasks.details:
Create comprehensive documentation for packages/core:

1. `packages/core/ARCHITECTURE.md`:
   - Bounded context map (Portfolio, Blog, Contact, Shared Kernel)
   - Dependency rules between contexts
   - Layer isolation rules (what core can/cannot import)
   - Visual diagram of context boundaries
   - Export structure (@repo/core/portfolio, @repo/core/shared, etc.)

2. `packages/core/GLOSSARY.md`:
   - Ubiquitous Language definitions for Portfolio context:
     - Project, Experience, Profile, Skill
     - Value Objects: Slug, Image, DateRange, LocalizedText, etc.
   - Term definitions with examples
   - Relationships between domain concepts

3. `packages/core/decisions/adr-template.md`:
```markdown
# ADR-XXX: [Title]

## Status
[Proposed | Accepted | Deprecated | Superseded]

## Context
[What is the issue we're facing?]

## Decision
[What did we decide?]

## Consequences
[What becomes easier or harder?]

## Alternatives Considered
[What other options did we evaluate?]
```

4. `packages/core/decisions/ADR-001-repository-interfaces-in-core.md`:
```markdown
# ADR-001: Repository Interfaces Belong in Core, Not Application

## Status
Accepted

## Context
In Clean Architecture, repository interfaces (ports) can be placed in either the domain layer (core) or application layer. We need to decide where they belong in our monorepo.

## Decision
Repository interfaces are defined in `packages/core` alongside their respective entities, not in `packages/application`.

Structure:
- `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/project/repositories/IProjectRepository.ts`
- `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/experience/repositories/IExperienceRepository.ts`

## Consequences

**Positive:**
- Interfaces live with their aggregate roots (high cohesion)
- Domain defines its own persistence contract
- Application layer doesn't need to define infrastructure contracts
- Easier to understand what operations are available per entity

**Negative:**
- Some Clean Architecture implementations put interfaces in application
- Requires clear documentation of this architectural choice

## Alternatives Considered

1. **Application Layer Interfaces**: Place in `packages/application/ports/repositories/`
   - Rejected: Separates interface from entity, lower cohesion

2. **Separate Ports Package**: Create `packages/ports`
   - Rejected: Over-engineering for monorepo, adds unnecessary complexity
```

Ensure documentation is referenced from root `CLAUDE.md`.
tasks.testStrategy:
Documentation validation:

1. Completeness:
   - ARCHITECTURE.md covers all bounded contexts
   - GLOSSARY.md defines all domain terms
   - ADR-001 follows template structure

2. Accuracy:
   - Visual diagrams match actual code structure
   - Export paths match package.json exports
   - Glossary definitions align with code

3. Formatting:
   - All markdown files render correctly
   - Links between documents work
   - Code blocks have proper syntax highlighting

4. Integration:
   - Reference added to root CLAUDE.md
   - Documentation discoverable by developers

5. Review:

   - No contradictions with CLAUDE.md guidelines

subtasks.details:
Create `packages/core/GLOSSARY.md` with structured definitions:

**Entities:**
- **Project**: A portfolio work item (commercial, personal, or academic)
- **Experience**: A professional work experience entry
- **Profile**: The portfolio owner's personal data
- **Skill**: A technical or professional capability

**Value Objects (from Shared Kernel):**
- **Slug**: URL-friendly identifier (kebab-case, min 3 chars)
- **Image**: Represents visual asset with URL, alt text, dimensions
- **DateRange**: Period with start/end dates and ongoing flag
- **LocalizedText**: Multi-language text content (pt-BR, en-US)
- **Markdown**: Rich text content in markdown format
- **Tag**: Categorization label
- **Technology**: Tech stack item with name and category

Include:
- Definition for each term
- TypeScript type signature examples
- Usage examples from actual entities
- Relationships between concepts (e.g., Project contains Skills, uses Slug)

```markdown
# ADR-XXX: [Title]

## Status

## Context

## Consequences

- [What becomes easier or better?]
```

subtasks.title: Write ADR-001 for repository interface placement decision
subtasks.description: Document architectural decision to place repository interfaces in core layer alongside entities rather than in application layer
subtasks.details:
Create `packages/core/decisions/ADR-001-repository-interfaces-in-core.md`:

**Status**: Accepted

**Context**: Explain that Clean Architecture allows repository interfaces (ports) in either domain or application layer. We needed to decide placement for monorepo.

**Decision**: Repository interfaces defined in `packages/core` alongside entities:
- `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/project/repositories/IProjectRepository.ts`
- `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/experience/repositories/IExperienceRepository.ts`
- Pattern: interfaces live with their aggregate roots

**Consequences**:
- **Positive**: High cohesion (interface + entity together), domain defines persistence contract, easier discoverability
- **Negative**: Differs from some Clean Architecture implementations, requires documentation

**Alternatives Considered**:
1. **Application Layer Interfaces** (`packages/application/ports/repositories/`): Rejected due to lower cohesion
2. **Separate Ports Package** (`packages/ports`): Rejected as over-engineering for monorepo

Reference actual repository interfaces from packages/core/src/portfolio.

tasks.title: Audit business validations delegated to Validator utility
tasks.description: Review all Value Objects and Entities in packages/core to ensure domain invariants reside in create() methods while technical format validations remain in Validator utility
tasks.details:
## Context

During Slug VO implementation (Task 5), a critical architectural boundary was established:
- **packages/utils/Validator**: purely technical format validations (isURL, isDateTime, isUUID, protocol checks)
- **packages/core VOs/Entities**: domain invariants and business rules (kebab-case requirement, locale mandatory, min/max lengths, enum memberships)

`Slug` and `LocalizedText` follow the correct pattern with all domain rules inline in `create()`. However, other VOs may have business logic delegated to Validator when it should be in the domain layer.

---

## Audit Scope

### Value Objects to Review

**packages/core/src/shared/vo/**:
- `Name.ts` - uses `Validator.alpha()` and `length(3, 100)` - **ISSUE: alphabetic constraint is domain rule**
- `Text.ts` - uses `Validator.length(min, max)` with domain-specific ranges - **ISSUE: length constraints are domain rules**
- `Url.ts` - uses `Validator.url()` - ✓ technical validation (protocol check)
- `DateTime.ts` - uses `Validator.datetime()` - ✓ technical validation (ISO format)
- `Id.ts` - uses `Validator.uuid()` - ✓ technical validation (UUID format)
- `EmploymentType.ts` - uses `Validator.in([...])` for enum membership - **ISSUE: enum membership is domain rule**
- `LocationType.ts` - uses `Validator.in([...])` for enum membership - **ISSUE: enum membership is domain rule**
- `SkillType.ts` - uses `Validator.in([...])` for enum membership - **ISSUE: enum membership is domain rule**
- `Fluency.ts` - uses `Validator.in([...])` for enum membership - **ISSUE: enum membership is domain rule**
- `Image.ts` - composes Url + LocalizedText - ✓ correct pattern
- `DateRange.ts` - start <= end check inline - ✓ correct pattern
- `Slug.ts` - kebab-case regex inline - ✓ **reference implementation**

**packages/core/src/shared/i18n/**:
- `LocalizedText.ts` - pt-BR mandatory rule inline - ✓ **reference implementation**

**packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/\*/model/**:
- `Profile.ts` (profile:57-64) - MAX_FEATURED_PROJECTS inline - ✓ correct pattern
- `ProfileStat.ts` (profile:30,33) - uses Text with min/max configs - depends on Text audit
- `Project.ts` (project:114) - uses Text with `{ min: 3, max: 500000 }` - depends on Text audit
- `Experience.ts` - composes VOs - depends on VO audits
- `ExperienceSkill.ts` - composes VOs - correct pattern
- `Skill.ts` (skill:37,40) - uses Text with custom configs - depends on Text audit

---

## Classification Criteria

### Technical Validations (KEEP in Validator)
- Format checks: `isUrl()`, `isDateTime()`, `isUUID()`
- Protocol requirements: `require_protocol: true`
- External standard compliance: ISO 8601, RFC 3986, UUID v4

### Domain Invariants (MOVE to VO/Entity)
- Business-specific constraints: min/max lengths tied to domain concepts
- Pattern requirements: kebab-case, alphabetic-only names
- Enum membership: allowed employment types, skill types, fluency levels
- Aggregate invariants: max featured projects, date range ordering
- Locale requirements: pt-BR mandatory

---

## Refactoring Strategy

### 1. Name VO (packages/core/src/shared/vo/Name.ts)

**Current (INCORRECT)**:
\`\`\`typescript
static create(value?: string): Either<ValidationError, Name> {
  const { error, isValid } = Validator.new(value)
    .alpha('The name must contain only letters.')
    .length(3, 100, 'The name must be between {{min}} and {{max}} characters.')
    .validate();
  if (!isValid && error) return left(new ValidationError({ code: Name.ERROR_CODE, message: error }));
  return right(new Name(value ?? ''));
}
\`\`\`

**Refactored (CORRECT)**:
\`\`\`typescript
static create(value?: string): Either<ValidationError, Name> {
  const trimmed = value?.trim();
  
  if (!trimmed || trimmed.length < 3 || trimmed.length > 100) {
    return left(new ValidationError({
      code: Name.ERROR_CODE,
      message: 'Name must be between 3 and 100 characters.'
    }));
  }
  
  if (!/^[A-ZÃÁÀÂÄÇÉÊËÍÏÕÓÔÖÚÜ\\s]+$/i.test(trimmed)) {
    return left(new ValidationError({
      code: Name.ERROR_CODE,
      message: 'Name must contain only letters and spaces.'
    }));
  }
  
  return right(new Name(trimmed));
}
\`\`\`

**Justification**: Alphabetic constraint is a domain rule for the Name concept, not a generic technical validation.

---

### 2. Text VO (packages/core/src/shared/vo/Text.ts)

**Current (INCORRECT)**:
\`\`\`typescript
static create(value?: string, config?: ITextConfig): Either<ValidationError, Text> {
  const { min = 3, max = 50 } = config ?? {};
  const { error, isValid } = Validator.new(value).length(min, max, '...').validate();
  if (!isValid && error) return left(new ValidationError({ code: Text.ERROR_CODE, message: error }));
  return right(new Text(value ?? '', config));
}
\`\`\`

**Refactored (CORRECT)**:
\`\`\`typescript
static create(value?: string, config?: ITextConfig): Either<ValidationError, Text> {
  const { min = 3, max = 50 } = config ?? {};
  const trimmed = value?.trim() ?? '';
  
  if (trimmed.length < min || trimmed.length > max) {
    return left(new ValidationError({
      code: Text.ERROR_CODE,
      message: `Text must be between ${min} and ${max} characters.`
    }));
  }
  
  return right(new Text(trimmed, config));
}
\`\`\`

**Justification**: Length constraints for domain-specific Text are business rules, not generic format checks.

---

### 3. Enum VOs (EmploymentType, LocationType, SkillType, Fluency)

**Current (INCORRECT)**:
\`\`\`typescript
static create(value: EmploymentTypeValue): Either<ValidationError, EmploymentType> {
  const { error, isValid } = Validator.new(value)
    .in([...EmploymentType.EMPLOYMENTS], 'The value must be a valid employment type.')
    .validate();
  if (!isValid && error) return left(new ValidationError({ code: EmploymentType.ERROR_CODE, message: error }));
  return right(new EmploymentType(value));
}
\`\`\`

**Refactored (CORRECT)**:
\`\`\`typescript
static create(value: EmploymentTypeValue): Either<ValidationError, EmploymentType> {
  if (!EmploymentType.EMPLOYMENTS.includes(value as any)) {
    return left(new ValidationError({
      code: EmploymentType.ERROR_CODE,
      message: `Invalid employment type. Must be one of: ${EmploymentType.EMPLOYMENTS.join(', ')}.`
    }));
  }
  return right(new EmploymentType(value));
}
\`\`\`

**Justification**: The set of valid employment types is a domain concept, not a generic format validator.

**Apply same pattern to**: LocationType, SkillType, Fluency.

---

### 4. Validator Utility - Keep Only Technical Validations

After refactoring, `packages/utils/src/validator/Validator.ts` should retain:
- `url()` - URL format and protocol validation (external standard RFC 3986)
- `datetime()` - ISO 8601 format validation (external standard)
- `uuid()` - UUID v4 format validation (external standard)
- `string()`, `nil()`, `notNil()` - basic type checks
- `gt()`, `gte()`, `lt()`, `lte()` - numeric comparisons (when used for technical thresholds)

**Remove or deprecate** (business logic, not technical format):
- `alpha()` - domain rule moved to Name VO
- `length()` - domain rule moved to Text VO and others
- `in()` - domain rule moved to enum VOs
- `empty()` - superseded by domain-specific trimming and validation

---

## Testing Impact

### Existing Tests Must Continue Passing

All current tests in `packages/core/test/**/*.test.ts` must pass after refactoring:
- Slug.test.ts - ✓ no changes needed (reference implementation)
- LocalizedText.test.ts - ✓ no changes needed (reference implementation)
- Name.test.ts - update to test inline validation logic
- Text.test.ts - update to test inline length checks
- EmploymentType.test.ts - update to test inline enum membership
- (same for LocationType, SkillType, Fluency)
- DateRange.test.ts - ✓ no changes needed (correct pattern)
- Profile.test.ts - ✓ no changes needed (correct pattern)

### New Test Requirements

After refactoring, verify:
1. All domain rules are covered by VO/Entity tests
2. Validator tests only cover technical format validations
3. Error messages are clear and domain-specific
4. No circular dependencies introduced

---

## Acceptance Criteria

### 1. All VOs and Entities Audited
- [ ] Every VO in `packages/core/src/shared/vo/` reviewed
- [ ] Every VO in `packages/core/src/shared/i18n/` reviewed
- [ ] Every Entity in `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/*/model/` reviewed
- [ ] Audit results documented (table of VO/Entity → Current validation → Classification → Action)

### 2. Domain Rules Extracted to Core Layer
- [ ] Name: alphabetic constraint moved inline
- [ ] Text: length constraints moved inline
- [ ] EmploymentType: enum membership moved inline
- [ ] LocationType: enum membership moved inline
- [ ] SkillType: enum membership moved inline
- [ ] Fluency: enum membership moved inline
- [ ] Any other misplaced rules identified and refactored

### 3. Validator Contains Only Technical Validations
- [ ] `isUrl()` - ✓ retained (RFC 3986)
- [ ] `isDateTime()` - ✓ retained (ISO 8601)
- [ ] `isUUID()` - ✓ retained (UUID v4)
- [ ] `alpha()`, `length()`, `in()` - removed or deprecated
- [ ] Validator documentation updated to clarify scope

### 4. All Tests Pass
- [ ] `pnpm test` in packages/core - all tests pass
- [ ] `pnpm test` in packages/utils - all tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests still work with refactored VOs
- [ ] No test coverage regression

### 5. Architecture Compliance
- [ ] No domain logic remains in packages/utils
- [ ] No external dependencies in packages/core (still zero framework imports)
- [ ] Either pattern consistently used for all domain errors
- [ ] Error codes and messages follow established patterns

---

## Implementation Notes

### Refactoring Order (Minimize Breakage)

1. **Audit first** - document all VOs/Entities and their current validation approach
2. **Start with leaf VOs** - Name, Text (no dependencies on other VOs)
3. **Then enum VOs** - EmploymentType, LocationType, SkillType, Fluency
4. **Update entities** - ProfileStat, Skill, Project (depend on Text)
5. **Run tests after each refactor** - ensure no regressions
6. **Update Validator documentation** - clarify technical-only scope

### Edge Cases to Consider

- **Text with extreme ranges**: Project content uses `{ min: 3, max: 500000 }` - this is a domain rule for project content length
- **Name normalization**: `normalized` and `capitalized` getters - keep these, they're domain behavior
- **Empty string handling**: ensure consistent trim() and empty checks across all VOs
- **Error message consistency**: follow Slug and LocalizedText patterns for error formatting

### References

- **Issue #314**: Original design boundary discussion
- **Task 5**: Slug VO implementation (reference pattern)
- **CLAUDE.md**: DDD rules and layer restrictions
- **docs/ARCHITECTURE.md**: Bounded context boundaries
- **docs/VALIDATION.md**: Edge validation vs domain invariants

tasks.title: Reorganize packages/core test folder architecture
tasks.description: Refactor test/ directory structure to mirror src/ layout, rename test/data/ to test/helpers/, reorganize builders and generators into subdirectories, and subdivide test/shared/ to match src/shared/ bounded context structure
tasks.details:
## Context

The current `packages/core/test/` structure diverges from `src/` and uses generic naming that obscures intent:
- `test/data/` contains both builders and data generators with no clear separation
- `test/data/bases/NameData.ts` has incorrect class name (`TextData`) diverging from file name
- `test/shared/` is flat with 14 files, not mirroring `src/shared/` subdirectories (base/, errors/, i18n/, vo/, types/)
- Entity-specific tests (experience/, project/, skill/) exist but lack consistent organization

## Migration Plan

### 1. Rename `test/data/` → `test/helpers/`

```bash
cd packages/core
git mv test/data test/helpers
```

### 2. Create subdirectories within `test/helpers/`

```bash
mkdir -p test/helpers/builders
mkdir -p test/helpers/generators
```

### 3. Move builders into `test/helpers/builders/`

Move all `*Builder.ts` files:
```bash
cd test/helpers
git mv EntityBuilder.ts builders/
git mv ExperienceBuilder.ts builders/
git mv LanguageBuilder.ts builders/
git mv ProfileBuilder.ts builders/
git mv ProfessionalValueBuilder.ts builders/
git mv ProjectBuilder.ts builders/
git mv SkillBuilder.ts builders/
git mv SocialNetworkBuilder.ts builders/
```

### 4. Move generators into `test/helpers/generators/`

Move all files from `bases/` subdirectory:
```bash
cd test/helpers
git mv bases/EmploymentTypeData.ts generators/
git mv bases/FluencyData.ts generators/
git mv bases/ImageData.ts generators/
git mv bases/LocationTypeData.ts generators/
git mv bases/SkillTypeData.ts generators/
git mv bases/SlugData.ts generators/
git mv bases/NameData.ts generators/TextData.ts  # Rename during move
rmdir bases/
```

### 5. Fix `TextData` class and exports

Update `test/helpers/generators/TextData.ts` (formerly NameData.ts):
- File already exports `TextData` class (correct)
- No code changes needed, just the file rename

Update `test/helpers/generators/index.ts`:
```typescript
export * from './EmploymentTypeData';
export * from './FluencyData';
export * from './ImageData';
export * from './LocationTypeData';
export * from './SkillTypeData';
export * from './SlugData';
export * from './TextData';  // Changed from NameData

export class Data {
  static employment = EmploymentTypeData;
  static fluency = FluencyData;
  static image = ImageData;
  static location = LocationTypeData;
  static skill = SkillTypeData;
  static slug = SlugData;
  static text = TextData;
}
```

### 6. Create `test/helpers/index.ts` barrel export

```typescript
export * from './builders';
export * from './generators';
```

Create `test/helpers/builders/index.ts`:
```typescript
export * from './EntityBuilder';
export * from './ExperienceBuilder';
export * from './LanguageBuilder';
export * from './ProfileBuilder';
export * from './ProfessionalValueBuilder';
export * from './ProjectBuilder';
export * from './SkillBuilder';
export * from './SocialNetworkBuilder';
```

### 7. Reorganize `test/shared/` to mirror `src/shared/`

Current flat structure with 14 files needs subdivision:

```bash
cd test/shared

# Already has: base/, errors/, i18n/, vo/ directories
# Move files into appropriate subdirectories:

# Base classes
git mv Entity.test.ts base/
git mv either.test.ts base/

# Error types
git mv errors.test.ts errors/

# Internationalization
git mv Locale.test.ts i18n/
git mv LocalizedText.test.ts i18n/

# Value Objects (remaining files)
git mv DateTime.test.ts vo/
git mv EmploymentType.test.ts vo/
git mv Fluency.test.ts vo/
git mv Id.test.ts vo/
git mv LocationType.test.ts vo/
git mv Name.test.ts vo/
git mv SkillType.test.ts vo/
git mv Text.test.ts vo/
git mv Url.test.ts vo/

# vo/ subdirectory already contains:
# - DateRange.test.ts
# - Image.test.ts
# - Slug.test.ts
```

After reorganization, `test/shared/` structure:
```
test/shared/
├── base/
│   ├── Entity.test.ts
│   └── either.test.ts
├── errors/
│   └── errors.test.ts
├── i18n/
│   ├── Locale.test.ts
│   └── LocalizedText.test.ts
└── vo/
    ├── DateRange.test.ts
    ├── DateTime.test.ts
    ├── EmploymentType.test.ts
    ├── Fluency.test.ts
    ├── Id.test.ts
    ├── Image.test.ts
    ├── LocationType.test.ts
    ├── Name.test.ts
    ├── SkillType.test.ts
    ├── Slug.test.ts
    ├── Text.test.ts
    └── Url.test.ts
```

This now mirrors `src/shared/` exactly:
```
src/shared/
├── base/
├── errors/
├── i18n/
├── types/
└── vo/
```

### 8. Update all import statements

Use find-replace across all test files:

```bash
# Builder imports
find test -name "*.test.ts" -exec sed -i "s|from '[./]*data'|from '../helpers'|g" {} \;
find test -name "*.test.ts" -exec sed -i "s|from '[./]*data/|from '../helpers/builders/|g" {} \;

# Generator imports (Data class)
find test -name "*.test.ts" -exec sed -i "s|from '[./]*data/bases'|from '../helpers/generators'|g" {} \;

# Builder file internal imports
find test/helpers/builders -name "*.ts" -exec sed -i "s|from './bases'|from '../generators'|g" {} \;
find test/helpers/builders -name "*.ts" -exec sed -i "s|from '../../src'|from '../../../src'|g" {} \;
```

Manual verification needed for:
- `test/helpers/builders/ProjectBuilder.ts:10` → `import { Data } from '../generators';`
- Any relative imports in entity test files referencing helpers

### 9. Final Directory Structure

```
packages/core/test/
├── helpers/
│   ├── builders/
│   │   ├── EntityBuilder.ts
│   │   ├── ExperienceBuilder.ts
│   │   ├── LanguageBuilder.ts
│   │   ├── ProfileBuilder.ts
│   │   ├── ProfessionalValueBuilder.ts
│   │   ├── ProjectBuilder.ts
│   │   ├── SkillBuilder.ts
│   │   ├── SocialNetworkBuilder.ts
│   │   └── index.ts
│   ├── generators/
│   │   ├── EmploymentTypeData.ts
│   │   ├── FluencyData.ts
│   │   ├── ImageData.ts
│   │   ├── LocationTypeData.ts
│   │   ├── SkillTypeData.ts
│   │   ├── SlugData.ts
│   │   ├── TextData.ts
│   │   └── index.ts
│   └── index.ts
├── shared/
│   ├── base/
│   │   ├── Entity.test.ts
│   │   └── either.test.ts
│   ├── errors/
│   │   └── errors.test.ts
│   ├── i18n/
│   │   ├── Locale.test.ts
│   │   └── LocalizedText.test.ts
│   └── vo/
│       ├── DateRange.test.ts
│       ├── DateTime.test.ts
│       ├── EmploymentType.test.ts
│       ├── Fluency.test.ts
│       ├── Id.test.ts
│       ├── Image.test.ts
│       ├── LocationType.test.ts
│       ├── Name.test.ts
│       ├── SkillType.test.ts
│       ├── Slug.test.ts
│       ├── Text.test.ts
│       └── Url.test.ts
├── experience/
│   ├── Experience.test.ts
│   └── ExperienceSkill.test.ts
├── language/
│   └── Language.test.ts
├── professional-value/
│   └── ProfessionalValue.test.ts
├── profile/
│   ├── Profile.test.ts
│   └── ProfileStat.test.ts
├── project/
│   └── Project.test.ts
├── skill/
│   ├── Skill.test.ts
│   └── SkillFactory.test.ts
└── social-network/
    └── SocialNetwork.test.ts
```

## Implementation Notes

- Use `git mv` for all file moves to preserve history
- Run `pnpm --filter @repo/core test` after each major step (builders, generators, shared subdivision)
- Use sed for bulk import updates, then manually verify critical files
- The `TextData` class name was already correct in NameData.ts; only file rename needed
- Some tests in `test/shared/base/`, `test/shared/errors/`, and `test/shared/i18n/` directories already exist; verify no duplicates before moving
- Priority: low (architectural cleanup, no functional changes)
tasks.testStrategy:
## Test Strategy

### 1. Pre-Migration Baseline

```bash
cd packages/core
pnpm test
```

**Success criteria:**
- All existing tests pass
- Capture test count and coverage baseline

### 2. Incremental Validation After Each Step

After each major refactoring step (rename data/, move builders, move generators, reorganize shared/):

```bash
pnpm test
```

**Success criteria:**
- Same number of test suites pass
- No new import errors
- No broken test references

### 3. Import Path Verification

```bash
# Check for any remaining old import paths
grep -r "from.*test/data" test/
grep -r "from.*data/bases" test/
grep -r "NameData" test/

# Should return no results
```

**Success criteria:**
- No references to old `test/data/` path
- No references to `test/data/bases/`
- No imports of `NameData` (should be `TextData`)

### 4. File Structure Verification

```bash
# Verify helpers/ structure
ls test/helpers/builders/*.ts | wc -l  # Should be 9 (8 builders + index)
ls test/helpers/generators/*.ts | wc -l  # Should be 8 (7 generators + index)

# Verify shared/ structure mirrors src/shared/
diff <(ls -d src/shared/*/ | xargs -n1 basename) \
     <(ls -d test/shared/*/ | xargs -n1 basename)

# Should show only 'types' in src/ (no tests yet)
```

**Success criteria:**
- All builders in `test/helpers/builders/`
- All generators in `test/helpers/generators/`
- `test/shared/` subdirectories match `src/shared/` (base, errors, i18n, vo)
- No orphaned files in old locations

### 5. Export Resolution Verification

```bash
# Test that barrel exports work correctly
cd test/helpers
node -e "console.log(Object.keys(require('./index.ts')))"

# Should include all builder and generator exports
```

**Success criteria:**
- All builders accessible via `test/helpers/builders`
- All generators accessible via `test/helpers/generators`
- `Data` class accessible via `test/helpers/generators`

### 6. TypeScript Compilation

```bash
pnpm types
```

**Success criteria:**
- No type errors
- All imports resolve correctly

### 7. Final Full Test Suite

```bash
pnpm test
pnpm test:coverage
```

**Success criteria:**
- All tests pass (same count as baseline)
- Coverage metrics unchanged or improved
- No skipped or pending tests introduced

### 8. Git History Verification

```bash
# Verify file moves preserved history
git log --follow test/helpers/builders/ProjectBuilder.ts
git log --follow test/helpers/generators/TextData.ts
```

**Success criteria:**
- Git history shows file was moved (not deleted/created)

### 9. Documentation Cross-Reference

- `CLAUDE.md` test organization rules

**Success criteria:**

tasks.title: Create and configure packages/application
tasks.description: Set up the foundational Application Layer package with all necessary configuration files, build pipeline integration, and ESLint architecture protection rules
tasks.details:
1. Create `packages/application/package.json` with:
   - name: '@repo/application'
   - dependencies: '@repo/core': 'workspace:*', '@repo/utils': 'workspace:*', 'uuid': '^10.0.0'
   - devDependencies: '@repo/eslint-config', '@repo/prettier-config', '@repo/typescript-config', '@types/uuid', '@vitest/coverage-v8', 'vitest'
   - scripts: test, test:watch, test:coverage, lint, lint:check, format, format:check, types
   - main: 'src/index.ts'
   - exports: '.': './src/index.ts', './portfolio': './src/portfolio/index.ts', './contact': './src/contact/index.ts'

2. Create `packages/application/tsconfig.json`:
   - extends: '@repo/typescript-config/base.json'
   - compilerOptions.baseUrl: 'src'
   - compilerOptions.paths: { '~/*': ['*'], '@repo/core/*': ['../../core/src/*'] }
   - include: ['src/**/*.ts', 'test/**/*.ts']

3. Create `packages/application/vitest.config.ts` (mirror packages/core/vitest.config.ts structure):
   - globals: true, environment: 'node'
   - include: ['test/**/*.test.ts']
   - coverage with v8 provider
   - alias '~' to './src'

4. Create `packages/application/src/index.ts` as barrel export:
   - export * from './portfolio'
   - export * from './contact'

5. Create `packages/eslint-config/application.js`:
   - extends library.js base config
   - add no-restricted-imports patterns for: ['react', 'react-dom', 'next', 'next/*', '@prisma/*', 'prisma', 'axios', 'node-fetch', 'resend']
   - error message: 'Application layer cannot import {framework} - violates Clean Architecture dependency rule'

6. Update `turbo.json` tasks.build to ensure application depends on core

7. Verify pnpm-workspace.yaml already includes 'packages/*'

8. Create directory structure: src/portfolio/, src/contact/, test/portfolio/, test/contact/
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run `pnpm install` from root and verify no errors
2. Run `pnpm -C packages/application types` - should pass with no TypeScript errors
3. Run `pnpm -C packages/application lint` - should pass
4. Verify ESLint rejects imports: attempt to import 'react' in a test file and confirm error
5. Run `turbo build --filter=@repo/application` - should build successfully
6. Verify barrel exports work: create test file importing from '@repo/application' and ensure resolution

subtasks.title: Create package.json with dependencies and scripts
subtasks.description: Create packages/application/package.json with complete dependency tree, workspace references, and all required npm scripts for testing, linting, formatting, and type checking
subtasks.details:
Create packages/application/package.json with:
- name: '@repo/application'
- dependencies: '@repo/core': 'workspace:*', '@repo/utils': 'workspace:*', 'uuid': '^10.0.0'
- devDependencies: '@repo/eslint-config': 'workspace:*', '@repo/prettier-config': 'workspace:*', '@repo/typescript-config': 'workspace:*', '@types/uuid': '^10.0.0', '@vitest/coverage-v8': '^2.0.0', 'vitest': '^2.0.0'
- scripts: { "test": "vitest run", "test:watch": "vitest", "test:coverage": "vitest run --coverage", "lint": "eslint src/ --fix", "lint:check": "eslint src/", "format": "prettier --write \"src/**/*.ts\"", "format:check": "prettier --check \"src/**/*.ts\"", "types": "tsc --noEmit" }
- main: 'src/index.ts'
- exports: { '.': './src/index.ts', './portfolio': './src/portfolio/index.ts', './contact': './src/contact/index.ts' }
- type: 'module'
- Verify pnpm-workspace.yaml includes 'packages/*' pattern
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm install` from monorepo root and verify no dependency resolution errors. Check that @repo/core and @repo/utils workspace dependencies are correctly linked. Verify package.json is valid JSON.

subtasks.title: Create tsconfig.json with path aliases and compiler options
subtasks.description: Configure TypeScript compilation settings extending the base config with proper path aliases for internal modules and core package references
subtasks.details:
Create packages/application/tsconfig.json with:
- extends: '@repo/typescript-config/base.json'
- compilerOptions: { "baseUrl": "src", "paths": { "~/*": ["*"], "@repo/core/*": ["../../core/src/*"], "@repo/utils/*": ["../../utils/src/*"] }, "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" }
- include: ["src/**/*.ts", "test/**/*.ts"]
- exclude: ["node_modules", "dist", "coverage"]
- Ensure strict mode and noUncheckedIndexedAccess are inherited from base config
- References should align with monorepo structure
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm -C packages/application types` and verify TypeScript can resolve path aliases. Create a test import statement using '~/' and '@repo/core/*' aliases to confirm path resolution works correctly.

subtasks.title: Create vitest.config.ts mirroring core test configuration
subtasks.description: Set up Vitest test runner configuration with coverage reporting, path aliases, and node environment matching the testing strategy established in packages/core
subtasks.details:
Create packages/application/vitest.config.ts with:
- import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config'
- import path from 'path'
- test.globals: true
- test.environment: 'node'
- test.include: ['test/**/*.test.ts']
- test.coverage: { provider: 'v8', reporter: ['text', 'json', 'html'], exclude: ['node_modules/', 'test/', 'dist/'] }
- resolve.alias: { '~': path.resolve(__dirname, './src'), '@repo/core': path.resolve(__dirname, '../core/src') }
- Mirror the exact structure from packages/core/vitest.config.ts to maintain consistency
- Ensure globals are enabled for describe/it/expect without imports
subtasks.testStrategy: Create a simple smoke test file at test/smoke.test.ts with a basic assertion. Run `pnpm -C packages/application test` and verify Vitest executes successfully. Run `pnpm -C packages/application test:coverage` and confirm coverage report is generated.

Run `pnpm -C packages/application lint:check` and verify it fails with the custom error message. Remove the test import and confirm lint passes.

subtasks.title: Update turbo.json build dependencies
subtasks.description: Configure Turborepo build pipeline to ensure packages/application build depends on packages/core completion, maintaining correct monorepo build order
subtasks.details:
Update turbo.json tasks configuration:
- Locate tasks.build configuration
- Ensure packages/application has dependsOn: ['^build'] to wait for dependencies (core, utils) to build first
- Verify outputs are correctly configured for caching: ['dist/**', '.turbo/**']
- The dependency chain should be: core → application → infra → web
- No changes needed if turbo.json already has correct ^build dependency pattern for all packages
- Test by running `turbo build --filter=@repo/application` and confirming core builds first
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm turbo build --filter=@repo/application --dry-run` and verify the task graph shows @repo/core builds before @repo/application. Run full build with `pnpm turbo build` from root and confirm no dependency errors occur. Check .turbo cache to verify application build artifacts are cached.

subtasks.title: Verify integration and run full validation suite
subtasks.description: Execute complete integration verification including workspace installation, TypeScript compilation, linting, and Turborepo build to confirm all configuration pieces work together correctly
subtasks.details:
Run complete validation sequence:
1. `pnpm install` from monorepo root - verify clean install with no errors
2. `pnpm -C packages/application types` - verify TypeScript compiles with no errors
3. `pnpm -C packages/application lint:check` - verify ESLint passes
4. `pnpm -C packages/application test` - verify test suite runs (even if only smoke test exists)
5. `pnpm turbo build --filter=@repo/application` - verify Turborepo build succeeds
6. Verify path aliases work by creating temporary test import: `import { Either } from '@repo/core/shared'` in a new file
7. Test ESLint architecture rules by attempting banned import and confirming rejection
8. Confirm pnpm-workspace.yaml includes 'packages/*' pattern
9. Document any issues found and ensure all configuration files are committed
subtasks.testStrategy: All validation steps listed in details serve as the test strategy. Create a checklist and mark each item as it passes. The task is complete when all 9 validation steps pass successfully. Document the results in task notes using `task-master update-subtask`.

subtasks.details:
Create `packages/application/src/portfolio/use-cases/GetFeaturedProjects.ts` with:

4. Add async execute(input: GetFeaturedProjectsInput): Promise<Either<DomainError, ProjectSummaryDTO[]>> method stub
5. Add private toDTO(project: Project, locale: Locale): ProjectSummaryDTO method stub
This establishes the use case pattern that will be reused across all subsequent use cases (Tasks 5-8).

No business logic tests yet - those come in subtask 4.

subtasks.title: Implement repository orchestration with findFeatured() and Either error handling
subtasks.description: Implement the execute() method body to call IProjectRepository.findFeatured() and handle errors using the Either pattern, wrapping repository exceptions in DomainError
subtasks.details:
In GetFeaturedProjects.execute():
1. Wrap repository call in try-catch block
2. Call `const projects = await this.projectRepository.findFeatured()`
3. In catch block, return `left(new DomainError('Failed to fetch featured projects'))` to convert infrastructure exceptions into domain errors
4. For now, return right([]) as placeholder until toDTO mapping is implemented in subtask 3
5. Ensure proper async/await error propagation
This establishes the error handling pattern for all use cases - repository failures are converted to Either.Left(DomainError) instead of throwing exceptions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Add tests to GetFeaturedProjects.test.ts: (1) Test repository success path - mock findFeatured() to return empty array, verify execute() returns Right([]), (2) Test repository error handling - mock findFeatured() to throw Error, verify execute() returns Left(DomainError) with correct message. Use Jest/Vitest expect().rejects or try-catch in async test.

subtasks.title: Implement toDTO() mapper for Project to ProjectSummaryDTO with locale handling
subtasks.description: Implement the private toDTO() method to transform Project entity to ProjectSummaryDTO, handling LocalizedText.get(locale) for all localized fields (title, caption, coverImage.alt, theme, skills)
subtasks.details:
In private toDTO(project: Project, locale: Locale): ProjectSummaryDTO:
1. Map id: project.id.toString() (ProjectId VO to string)
2. Map slug: project.slug.toString() (Slug VO to string)
3. Map title: project.title.get(locale) (LocalizedText to string)
4. Map caption: project.caption.get(locale) (LocalizedText to string)
5. Map coverImage object: { url: project.coverImage.url.toString(), alt: project.coverImage.alt.get(locale) } (nested object with URL VO and localized alt)
6. Map theme: project.theme?.get(locale) (optional LocalizedText, handle null/undefined)
7. Map skills: project.skills.map(s => s.name.get(locale)) (array transformation with locale)
8. Map publishedAt: project.period.start.toISOString() (DateRange VO to ISO string)
Integrate into execute(): replace right([]) with `const dtos = projects.map(p => this.toDTO(p, input.locale)); return right(dtos);`
subtasks.testStrategy: Add comprehensive DTO mapping tests: (1) Create mock Project entity with all fields populated, (2) Verify toDTO() returns object matching ProjectSummaryDTO shape, (3) Test all 8 fields are mapped correctly (id, slug, title, caption, coverImage.url, coverImage.alt, theme, skills, publishedAt), (4) Test locale handling - call toDTO with 'pt-BR' and 'en', verify LocalizedText fields return correct translations, (5) Test optional theme field when null/undefined.

subtasks.title: Create GetProjectBySlug use case file with structure and dependencies
subtasks.description: Create the use case file packages/application/src/portfolio/use-cases/GetProjectBySlug.ts with all necessary imports, interfaces, and class structure including constructor with IProjectRepository dependency injection
subtasks.details: Create file packages/application/src/portfolio/use-cases/GetProjectBySlug.ts. Import Either, left, right from @repo/core/shared. Import DomainError, NotFoundError, ValidationError from @repo/core/shared. Import Locale, Slug from @repo/core/shared. Import IProjectRepository, Project from @repo/core/portfolio. Import ProjectDetailDTO from ../dtos/ProjectDetailDTO. Import ProjectSummaryDTO from ../dtos/ProjectSummaryDTO. Define GetProjectBySlugInput interface with slug: string and locale: Locale properties. Create GetProjectBySlug class with constructor accepting private readonly projectRepository: IProjectRepository. Define execute method signature returning Promise<Either<NotFoundError | ValidationError | DomainError, ProjectDetailDTO>>. This establishes the foundation for the use case following Clean Architecture dependency injection patterns.

subtasks.title: Implement execute method with slug validation and NotFoundError handling
subtasks.description: Implement the execute method logic including slug validation using Slug.create() with early return on validation error, repository findBySlug call, and NotFoundError when project not found
subtasks.details: Inside execute method: Call Slug.create(input.slug) and store result. Check if slugResult.isLeft() - if true, return left(slugResult.value) for early ValidationError handling. Declare slugResult.value as the validated slug. Call await this.projectRepository.findBySlug(slugResult.value) and store in project variable. Check if project is null - if true, return left(new NotFoundError(`Project with slug '${input.slug}' not found`)). This implements the two critical error paths: ValidationError from invalid slug format and NotFoundError when project doesn't exist in repository. Both use Either pattern for type-safe error handling without throwing exceptions.

subtasks.title: Implement related projects orchestration and final response
subtasks.description: Add related projects fetching using findRelated repository method, map to ProjectSummaryDTO array, and return successful Either with complete ProjectDetailDTO
subtasks.details: After project is found and validated: Call await this.projectRepository.findRelated(project.id, 3) to fetch up to 3 related projects. Store result in relatedProjects variable. Map relatedProjects array using this.toSummaryDTO(p, input.locale) for each project p. Store mapped array in relatedDTOs variable. Call this.toDetailDTO(project, input.locale, relatedDTOs) to transform the main project. Return right() wrapping the ProjectDetailDTO result. This completes the happy path orchestration with two repository calls (findBySlug for main project, findRelated for related projects) and demonstrates Clean Architecture repository composition pattern.

subtasks.title: Implement toDetailDTO and toSummaryDTO private mapper methods
subtasks.description: Create two private DTO mapper methods: toDetailDTO for full project details with all 12+ fields including optional fields, and toSummaryDTO for related projects summary with 8 fields
subtasks.details: Create private toDetailDTO(project: Project, locale: Locale, relatedProjects: ProjectSummaryDTO[]): ProjectDetailDTO method. Map all fields: id (project.id.toString()), slug (project.slug.toString()), title (project.title.get(locale)), caption (project.caption.get(locale)), coverImage object with url and alt, theme (project.theme?.get(locale) for optional), skills array mapping s.name.get(locale), publishedAt (project.period.start.toISOString()), content (project.content.toString()), summary (project.summary?.get(locale)), objectives (project.objectives?.get(locale)), role (project.role?.get(locale)), team (project.team), period object with startAt and endAt (optional with ?.), relatedProjects array. Create private toSummaryDTO(project: Project, locale: Locale): ProjectSummaryDTO method with 8 fields: id, slug, title, caption, coverImage, theme, skills, publishedAt. Handle optional fields with ?. operator for type safety.

subtasks.title: Create GetProfile use case class with repository DI and execute() method
subtasks.description: Implement the GetProfile use case class with IProfileRepository dependency injection, GetProfileInput interface, and execute() method that calls repository.find() and handles NotFoundError when profile is null
subtasks.details:
Create `packages/application/src/portfolio/use-cases/GetProfile.ts` with:
- Import Either, left, right from @repo/core/shared
- Import NotFoundError, Locale from @repo/core/shared
- Import IProfileRepository, Profile from @repo/core/portfolio
- Import ProfileDTO, ProfileStatDTO, SocialNetworkDTO from ../dtos
- Define GetProfileInput interface with locale: Locale field
- Create GetProfile class with constructor accepting IProfileRepository
- Implement async execute() method that:
  - Calls await this.profileRepository.find()
  - Returns left(new NotFoundError('Profile not found')) if profile is null
  - Returns right(this.toDTO(profile, input.locale)) on success
- Return type: Promise<Either<NotFoundError, ProfileDTO>>
subtasks.testStrategy: Test execute() method with mocked IProfileRepository: (1) Happy path - repository returns Profile, execute returns Right(ProfileDTO), (2) NotFoundError path - repository returns null, execute returns Left(NotFoundError with 'Profile not found' message)

subtasks.title: Implement toDTO() mapper for Profile entity with 7 top-level fields and 3 nested arrays
subtasks.description: Create the main toDTO() private method that maps Profile domain entity to ProfileDTO, handling all 7 top-level fields (id, name, headline, bio, photo, stats, featuredProjectSlugs, socialNetworks) including 3 array transformations with proper locale handling
subtasks.details:
Implement private toDTO(profile: Profile, locale: Locale): ProfileDTO method:
- Map id: profile.id.toString()
- Map name: profile.name.toString()
- Map headline: profile.headline.get(locale)
- Map bio: profile.bio.get(locale)
- Map photo object: { url: profile.photo.url.toString(), alt: profile.photo.alt.get(locale) }
- Map stats array: profile.stats.map(s => this.toStatDTO(s, locale))
- Map featuredProjectSlugs array: profile.featuredProjectSlugs.map(slug => slug.toString())
- Map socialNetworks array: profile.socialNetworks.map(sn => this.toSocialNetworkDTO(sn))
- Return complete ProfileDTO object with all 7 fields
subtasks.testStrategy: Test toDTO() mapping completeness: (1) Verify all 7 top-level fields are correctly mapped, (2) Test locale handling for headline, bio, and photo.alt, (3) Test non-localized fields (id, name) use toString(), (4) Verify 3 array transformations produce correct lengths and call respective mapper methods

subtasks.title: Implement toStatDTO() and toSocialNetworkDTO() mapper methods
subtasks.description: Create two private mapper methods: toStatDTO() for ProfileStat with mixed locale/non-locale handling (label and value are localized, icon is not), and toSocialNetworkDTO() for SocialNetwork with all non-localized fields
subtasks.details:
Implement two private mapper methods:

1. toStatDTO(stat: ProfileStat, locale: Locale): ProfileStatDTO
   - Map label: stat.label.get(locale) — localized
   - Map value: stat.value.get(locale) — localized
   - Map icon: stat.icon — not localized, direct access
   - Return ProfileStatDTO object

2. toSocialNetworkDTO(sn: SocialNetwork): SocialNetworkDTO
   - Map id: sn.id.toString() — not localized
   - Map name: sn.name.toString() — not localized
   - Map url: sn.url.toString() — not localized
   - Return SocialNetworkDTO object

Both methods should handle the different localization patterns correctly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test mapper methods: (1) toStatDTO() - verify locale handling for label and value fields, verify icon field is not localized, (2) toSocialNetworkDTO() - verify all fields use toString() and are not localized, (3) Test with multiple stats and social networks to ensure array mapping works correctly
