subtasks.description: Implement the SendContactMessage use case class with IEmailService dependency injection, SendContactMessageInput interface, and comprehensive input validation for name, email, and message fields
subtasks.details: Create `packages/application/src/contact/use-cases/SendContactMessage.ts` with: (1) Import Either, left, right, DomainError, ValidationError from @repo/core/shared, (2) Import IEmailService port and ContactMessageDTO, (3) Define SendContactMessageInput interface with name, email, message string properties, (4) Implement SendContactMessage class with constructor accepting IEmailService, (5) Implement execute() method signature returning Promise<Either<ValidationError | DomainError, void>>, (6) Add validation logic: name required with trim check, email required with regex validation (/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/), message required with trim check, (7) Return left(ValidationError) with descriptive message for each validation failure, (8) Construct ContactMessageDTO with trimmed values after all validations pass, (9) Call emailService.send(dto) and propagate errors, (10) Return right(undefined) on success
subtasks.testStrategy: Create comprehensive test suite in `packages/application/test/contact/SendContactMessage.test.ts` covering: (1) Mock IEmailService.send() to return right(undefined), (2) Happy path: valid input returns Right(void) and calls emailService.send() exactly once, (3) Empty name validation: returns Left(ValidationError) with 'Name is required' message, (4) Whitespace-only name: returns Left(ValidationError), (5) Empty email validation: returns Left(ValidationError) with 'Valid email is required', (6) Invalid email formats: test no @ symbol, no domain, @-only, multiple @ symbols, (7) Empty message validation: returns Left(ValidationError) with 'Message is required', (8) Whitespace-only message: returns Left(ValidationError), (9) Verify trimming: input with leading/trailing spaces creates DTO with trimmed values, (10) Email service error propagation: mock returns Left(DomainError), verify use case propagates it, (11) Verify emailService.send() is NOT called when any validation fails

subtasks.title: Implement validation error scenarios and trimming logic
subtasks.description: Add detailed validation logic for all input fields with proper error messages and trimming behavior, ensuring early returns on validation failures before calling the email service
subtasks.details: In SendContactMessage.execute() method: (1) Implement name validation first: check for falsy, empty string after trim, return left(new ValidationError('Name is required')) on failure, (2) Implement email validation second: check for falsy or empty after trim, apply regex test /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/, return left(new ValidationError('Valid email is required')) on failure, (3) Implement message validation third: check for falsy, empty string after trim, return left(new ValidationError('Message is required')) on failure, (4) Only after ALL validations pass, construct ContactMessageDTO with input.name.trim(), input.email.trim(), input.message.trim(), (5) Ensure validation order is sequential with early returns - this prevents emailService.send() from being called when validation fails, (6) Add comments documenting each validation rule for clarity
subtasks.testStrategy: Extend test suite with edge cases: (1) Test name with only spaces/tabs/newlines returns ValidationError, (2) Test email with spaces in middle (e.g., 'user @domain.com') returns ValidationError, (3) Test email with missing TLD (e.g., 'user@domain') returns ValidationError, (4) Test email with special characters in invalid positions, (5) Test message with only whitespace characters returns ValidationError, (6) Test that validation happens in order: if name AND email are invalid, name error is returned first, (7) Use Jest spies to verify emailService.send() call count is 0 when validation fails, (8) Test input with mixed valid/invalid fields to ensure all validations are checked independently

subtasks.title: Implement IEmailService orchestration and error propagation
subtasks.description: Add email service integration that constructs the DTO, calls the port's send method, and properly propagates both validation errors and domain errors from the service
subtasks.details: In SendContactMessage.execute() after validation passes: (1) Construct ContactMessageDTO object with properties: name (trimmed), email (trimmed), message (trimmed), (2) Call await this.emailService.send(dto) and capture result in Either variable, (3) Check if result.isLeft() - if true, return left(result.value) to propagate the error (could be DomainError from email service), (4) If result.isRight(), return right(undefined) to indicate successful execution, (5) Ensure type safety: execute() return type is Promise<Either<ValidationError | DomainError, void>> which allows both validation errors (from use case) and domain errors (from email service), (6) Add JSDoc comments explaining the error propagation flow
subtasks.testStrategy: Test IEmailService integration: (1) Mock emailService.send() to return right(undefined), verify use case returns right(undefined), (2) Mock emailService.send() to return left(new DomainError('SMTP connection failed')), verify use case propagates the exact error, (3) Mock emailService.send() to return left(new DomainError('Rate limit exceeded')), verify propagation, (4) Verify emailService.send() is called with exact DTO structure: {name: 'trimmed', email: 'trimmed', message: 'trimmed'}, (5) Use Jest mock.calls to verify DTO passed to send() has no leading/trailing whitespace, (6) Test multiple successful executions to ensure no state pollution between calls, (7) Verify that when validation fails, emailService.send() spy shows 0 invocations

subtasks.title: Create barrel exports and comprehensive test suite
subtasks.description: Set up proper barrel exports for the use case and create a comprehensive test suite covering all validation scenarios, error propagation, and edge cases
subtasks.details: Create exports and tests: (1) Create `packages/application/src/contact/use-cases/index.ts` with single export: export { SendContactMessage } from './SendContactMessage', (2) Update `packages/application/src/contact/index.ts` to include: export * from './use-cases' (alongside existing exports from ./ports and ./dtos), (3) Create comprehensive test file `packages/application/test/contact/SendContactMessage.test.ts` with describe block for SendContactMessage, (4) Set up beforeEach with fresh mock IEmailService instance, (5) Implement 10+ test cases covering: happy path, empty name, whitespace name, empty email, invalid email formats (6 variations), empty message, whitespace message, trimming verification, service error propagation, validation prevents service call, (6) Use Jest matchers: expect(result.isLeft()).toBe(true), expect(result.value).toBeInstanceOf(ValidationError), expect(result.value.message).toContain('required'), (7) Run test suite and verify 100% coverage of SendContactMessage.ts
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate test suite completeness: (1) Run `pnpm test SendContactMessage.test.ts` and verify all tests pass, (2) Check coverage report shows 100% line coverage for SendContactMessage.ts, (3) Verify test names follow 'should <expected> when <context>' pattern, (4) Ensure each test case is isolated with fresh mocks, (5) Verify test execution time is reasonable (<100ms for entire suite), (6) Run tests in watch mode and verify no flakiness, (7) Test should cover both branches of every if statement (validation pass/fail), (8) Verify mock assertions use .toHaveBeenCalledWith() for exact DTO verification, (9) Run `pnpm test` at monorepo root to ensure turbo integration works, (10) Verify no console warnings or errors during test execution

tasks.expansionPrompt: Implement SendContactMessage use case with comprehensive input validation: (1) Create use case class with IEmailService DI and SendContactMessageInput interface, implement execute() method signature returning Either<ValidationError | DomainError, void>, (2) Implement validation logic for name (required, non-whitespace), email (required, regex format validation), and message (required, non-whitespace) with early returns on ValidationError, (3) Implement DTO construction with trimmed values and emailService.send() orchestration with error propagation, (4) Create comprehensive test suite covering: happy path with successful send, 6+ validation error scenarios (empty name, whitespace-only name, empty email, invalid email formats, empty message, whitespace-only message), trimming verification, email service error propagation, and verification that emailService.send() is NOT called when validation fails.

tasks.title: Refactor Validator to use Zod internally and update all Value Objects
tasks.description: Refactor the Validator class in packages/utils to use Zod as its internal validation engine, rename Validator.new to Validator.of, add new methods (.regex, .refine, .notEmpty), and update all 9 Value Objects in packages/core that use Validator.new while preserving the external API.
tasks.testStrategy:
### Testing Strategy

#### 1. Unit Tests for Validator Class (packages/utils/test/node/Validator.test.ts)

**Existing Tests - Must Pass:**
- All existing test suites for `.alpha()`, `.length()`, `.url()`, `.uuid()`, `.string()`, `.nil()`, `.notNil()`, `.empty()`, `.datetime()`, `.in()`, `.lt()`, `.lte()`, `.gt()`, `.gte()`
- `Validator.combine()` static method tests
- Mustache template rendering test (`.length()` with `{{min}}` and `{{max}}`)
- Verify all tests work with both `Validator.of()` and `Validator.new()`

**New Tests - Must Add:**
- `.regex()`: valid pattern match, invalid pattern match
- `.refine()`: predicate returns true, predicate returns false
- `.notEmpty()`: non-empty string, empty string, whitespace-only string
- Chaining new methods with existing methods (e.g., `.string().notEmpty().regex()`)

**Run Command:**
```bash
pnpm -C packages/utils test Validator.test.ts
```

#### 2. Integration Tests for Value Objects (packages/core)

**Test All 9 VOs:**
- Run existing VO test suites to ensure no regression:
  - `packages/core/test/shared/vo/Id.test.ts`
  - `packages/core/test/shared/vo/Text.test.ts`
  - `packages/core/test/shared/vo/Url.test.ts`
  - `packages/core/test/shared/vo/DateTime.test.ts`
  - `packages/core/test/shared/vo/Name.test.ts`
  - `packages/core/test/shared/vo/EmploymentType.test.ts`
  - `packages/core/test/shared/vo/Fluency.test.ts`
  - `packages/core/test/shared/vo/SkillType.test.ts`
  - `packages/core/test/shared/vo/LocationType.test.ts`

**Validation Criteria:**
- All existing VO tests pass without modification
- Error messages remain identical
- `{ isValid, error }` structure unchanged
- Either pattern (Left/Right) still works correctly

**Run Command:**
```bash
pnpm -C packages/core test
```

#### 3. Type Checking

**Verify TypeScript Compilation:**
```bash
pnpm -C packages/utils types
pnpm -C packages/core types
```

**Expected Outcome:**
- No type errors in either package
- Generic type inference `Validator<TValue>` still works
- VO creation returns correct Either types

#### 4. Full Monorepo Test Suite

**Run All Tests:**
```bash
pnpm test
```

**Expected Outcome:**
- All tests in `packages/utils`, `packages/core`, and `packages/application` pass
- No breaking changes in dependent packages
- Coverage remains at or above current levels

#### 5. Manual Verification Checklist

- [ ] Validator exports correctly from `@repo/utils/validator`
- [ ] All 9 VOs use `Validator.of()` instead of `Validator.new()`
- [ ] Error messages from VOs match exactly (character-by-character)
- [ ] `.regex()`, `.refine()`, `.notEmpty()` methods are available and documented
- [ ] Mustache template rendering works (test with Text VO length validation)
- [ ] First-error behavior preserved (validation stops at first failure)
- [ ] `Validator.combine()` still works for multi-validator scenarios
- [ ] Zod is listed in `packages/utils/package.json` dependencies

#### 6. Edge Case Testing

**Test Scenarios:**
- Empty string validation with `.notEmpty()` vs `.empty()`
- Chaining multiple validations (first error wins)
- Null/undefined handling in `.nil()` and `.notNil()`
- Regex pattern with special characters
- Custom refine predicate with complex logic
- Mustache template with missing placeholders

#### 7. Performance Verification

**Before/After Comparison:**
- Run VO creation benchmarks (if available)
- Ensure Zod schema compilation doesn't introduce significant overhead
- Validate that validation chains execute in O(n) time (n = number of validations)

#### 8. Documentation Check

**Verify:**
- JSDoc comments on new methods (`.regex()`, `.refine()`, `.notEmpty()`)
- Updated exports in index files
- No breaking changes documented (if any)

### Success Criteria

✅ All existing tests pass without modification  
✅ New tests for `.regex()`, `.refine()`, `.notEmpty()` pass  
✅ All 9 VOs updated and tested  
✅ Type checking passes in all packages  
✅ Full monorepo test suite passes  
✅ Error messages identical to previous implementation  
✅ External API `{ isValid, error }` unchanged  
✅ GitHub issue #329 requirements met

tasks.title: Migrate Slug, LocalizedText, DateRange and Profile entity to use refactored Validator
tasks.description: Replace manual if-guard validation in Slug, LocalizedText, DateRange, and Profile with the new Validator.of() fluent API, using .regex(), .refine(), and .notEmpty() methods while preserving all ERROR_CODEs and error messages.
tasks.testStrategy:
### Testing Strategy

#### 1. Run Existing Test Suites (Must Pass Without Modification)

Run the following test files to verify behavior preservation:

```bash
# Test Slug migration
pnpm -C packages/core test packages/core/test/shared/vo/Slug.test.ts

# Test LocalizedText migration  
pnpm -C packages/core test packages/core/test/shared/i18n/LocalizedText.test.ts

# Test DateRange migration
pnpm -C packages/core test packages/core/test/shared/vo/DateRange.test.ts

# Test Profile migration
pnpm -C packages/core test packages/core/test/profile/Profile.test.ts

# Run all core tests
pnpm -C packages/core test
```

**Success Criteria:**
- All existing tests must pass without modification
- No new test failures introduced
- Coverage percentage remains the same or increases

---

#### 2. Verify Error Messages and Codes

**Manual Verification Checklist:**

For **Slug**:
- [ ] Empty/undefined input returns ERROR_CODE='INVALID_SLUG' with message 'Slug must be at least 3 characters.'
- [ ] Short slug (2 chars) returns ERROR_CODE='INVALID_SLUG' with message 'Slug must be at least 3 characters.'
- [ ] Invalid pattern returns ERROR_CODE='INVALID_SLUG' with message 'Slug must be kebab-case (lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens only).'
- [ ] Valid slug normalizes to lowercase and returns Right

For **LocalizedText**:
- [ ] Missing pt-BR returns ERROR_CODE='INVALID_LOCALIZED_TEXT' with message 'pt-BR is required and must be non-empty after trim.'
- [ ] Empty pt-BR returns ERROR_CODE='INVALID_LOCALIZED_TEXT' with message 'pt-BR is required and must be non-empty after trim.'
- [ ] Valid input returns Right with normalized value

For **DateRange**:
- [ ] Invalid start date returns DateTime.ERROR_CODE (propagated)
- [ ] Invalid end date returns DateTime.ERROR_CODE (propagated)
- [ ] start > end returns ERROR_CODE='INVALID_DATE_RANGE' with message 'Start date must be before or equal to end date.'
- [ ] Valid range returns Right

For **Profile**:
- [ ] >6 featured projects returns ERROR_CODE='TOO_MANY_FEATURED_PROJECTS' with message 'Maximum 6 featured projects allowed, got X.'
- [ ] ≤6 featured projects continues to Name validation

---

#### 3. TypeScript Type Checking

```bash
# Verify no type errors introduced
pnpm -C packages/core types
```

**Success Criteria:**
- No new TypeScript errors
- All imports resolve correctly
- Validator methods are correctly typed

---

#### 4. Integration Test with Application Layer

Since these Value Objects are used in Use Cases (Task 8, 6, 7, etc.), verify:

```bash
# Run application layer tests if available
pnpm -C packages/application test
```

**Success Criteria:**
- No Use Case tests fail due to changed Value Object behavior
- Error propagation still works correctly in Either chains

---

#### 5. Regression Testing Checklist

**For Each Modified File:**
- [ ] Import statement added for Validator
- [ ] ERROR_CODE constant preserved
- [ ] Error messages match exactly (character-for-character)
- [ ] Normalization logic preserved (trim, toLowerCase, etc.)
- [ ] Return type is Either<ValidationError, T>
- [ ] Both happy path and error path tested
- [ ] No console.log or debugging code left behind

---

#### 6. Build Verification

```bash
# Build the entire monorepo
pnpm build

# Verify packages/core builds successfully
pnpm -C packages/core build
```

**Success Criteria:**
- Clean build with no errors
- No build warnings introduced
- Dist files generated correctly

---

#### 7. Manual Smoke Test (Optional)

Create a temporary test file to verify end-to-end:

```typescript
// packages/core/test/migration-smoke-test.ts
import { Slug, LocalizedText, DateRange } from '../src/shared';
import { Profile } from '../src/portfolio';

describe('Migration Smoke Test', () => {
  it('Slug validation works with new Validator', () => {
    expect(Slug.create('valid-slug').isRight()).toBe(true);
    expect(Slug.create('ab').isLeft()).toBe(true);
  });
  
  it('LocalizedText validation works with new Validator', () => {
    expect(LocalizedText.create({ 'pt-BR': 'Texto' }).isRight()).toBe(true);
    expect(LocalizedText.create({ 'pt-BR': '' }).isLeft()).toBe(true);
  });
  
  it('DateRange validation works with new Validator', () => {
    expect(DateRange.create('2024-01-01', '2024-12-31').isRight()).toBe(true);
    expect(DateRange.create('2024-12-31', '2024-01-01').isLeft()).toBe(true);
  });
  
  it('Profile validation works with new Validator', () => {
    const props = {
      id: '1',
      name: 'John',
      headline: { 'pt-BR': 'Dev' },
      bio: { 'pt-BR': 'Bio' },
      photo: { url: 'http://example.com/photo.jpg', alt: { 'pt-BR': 'Photo' } },
      stats: [],
      featuredProjectSlugs: ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd', 'e', 'f', 'g'], // 7 items
    };
    expect(Profile.create(props).isLeft()).toBe(true);
  });
});
```

Delete this file after verification.

---

## Final Checklist Before Marking Task Complete

- [ ] All 4 files modified
- [ ] All imports added
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Build succeeds
- [ ] No behavior changes detected
- [ ] Error codes and messages preserved
- [ ] Code reviewed for manual if-guards (none should remain for migrated validations)
- [ ] GitHub issue #330 referenced in commit message

tasks.title: Refactor documentation — unify, deduplicate and reorganize all .md files
tasks.description: Consolidate all project documentation into a single, unified, numbered hierarchy (docs/00-INTRODUCTION.md through docs/10-GLOSSARY.md), create a comprehensive INDEX.md as the navigation entry point, and slim down CLAUDE.md to ~200 lines by removing duplicated content and linking to the new documentation structure.
tasks.details:
## Overview

The current documentation is fragmented across multiple locations with significant duplication:
- CLAUDE.md: 635 lines (contains full architecture, DDD patterns, testing rules, etc.)
- docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: 164 lines
- docs/BOUNDED_CONTEXTS.md: 108 lines
- docs/GLOSSARY.md: 126 lines
- packages/core/GLOSSARY.md: 285 lines (duplicate concept)
- packages/core/ARCHITECTURE.md (duplicate concept)
- docs/README.md (simple index, not comprehensive)

This task unifies all documentation under a single numbered hierarchy with **exactly one authoritative location per concept**.

---

## Target Documentation Structure

```text
docs/
  INDEX.md                    → Single navigation entry point with complete TOC
  00-INTRODUCTION.md          → Project overview, vision, tech stack summary
  01-GETTING-STARTED.md       → Setup, installation, development workflow
  02-ARCHITECTURE.md          → Clean Architecture, dependency rule, layers
  03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md      → DDD contexts, aggregates, shared kernel
  04-APPLICATION-LAYER.md     → Use cases, ports, DTOs (merge APPLICATION.md)
  05-API-CONTRACTS.md         → API envelope, error mapping (merge API.md + ERROR_HANDLING.md)
  06-VALIDATION.md            → Edge validation vs domain invariants
  07-I18N.md                  → Internationalization strategy
  08-TESTING.md               → Testing strategy, builders, coverage
  09-PATTERNS.md              → DDD patterns (Either, VO, Entity templates), GoF patterns
  10-GLOSSARY.md              → Unified ubiquitous language (merge both GLOSSARY.md files)

packages/core/
  README.md                   → Links to docs/INDEX.md, explains Core's role
  decisions/                  → Keep ADRs here (already good structure)
    ADR-001-repository-interfaces-in-core.md
    adr-template.md

CLAUDE.md                     → Slimmed to ~200 lines (see below)
```

---

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Create docs/INDEX.md (~100 lines)

Create comprehensive navigation entry point:

```markdown
# Portfolio Monorepo — Documentation Index

> **Single source of truth for all project documentation.**  
> All internal links in this index are tested and guaranteed to resolve.

## Quick Navigation

- New to the project? Start with [00-INTRODUCTION](./00-INTRODUCTION.md)
- Setting up locally? See [01-GETTING-STARTED](./01-GETTING-STARTED.md)
- Understanding architecture? Read [02-ARCHITECTURE](./02-ARCHITECTURE.md) → [03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS](./03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md)
- Looking for a term? Jump to [10-GLOSSARY](./10-GLOSSARY.md)

## Complete Documentation Map

### Foundation (00-03)
- **[00-INTRODUCTION](./00-INTRODUCTION.md)** — Project vision, goals, tech stack overview
- **[01-GETTING-STARTED](./01-GETTING-STARTED.md)** — Installation, monorepo setup, dev workflow, Task Master integration
- **[02-ARCHITECTURE](./02-ARCHITECTURE.md)** — Clean Architecture, dependency rule, layer responsibilities
- **[03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS](./03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md)** — DDD contexts (Portfolio, Blog, Contact), aggregates, shared kernel

### Application & Interface (04-07)
- **[04-APPLICATION-LAYER](./04-APPLICATION-LAYER.md)** — Use cases, ports, DTOs, orchestration patterns
- **[05-API-CONTRACTS](./05-API-CONTRACTS.md)** — API envelope, error codes, HTTP mapping, i18n at edge
- **[06-VALIDATION](./06-VALIDATION.md)** — Edge validation (Zod) vs domain invariants (Either pattern)
- **[07-I18N](./07-I18N.md)** — UI translations, domain content localization, locale routing

### Quality & Patterns (08-10)
- **[08-TESTING](./08-TESTING.md)** — Testing strategy, builders, coverage criteria, TDD workflow
- **[09-PATTERNS](./09-PATTERNS.md)** — DDD implementation patterns (Either, VO, Entity), GoF patterns
- **[10-GLOSSARY](./10-GLOSSARY.md)** — Ubiquitous language, architectural terms, domain vocabulary

## Cross-References

- **CLAUDE.md** (root) — Operational quick reference, links to this index
- **packages/core/README.md** — Core package overview, links to this index
- **packages/core/decisions/** — Architectural Decision Records (ADRs)
- **ROADMAP.md** — Product roadmap and implementation phases

## Documentation Principles

1. **Single Source of Truth** — Each concept appears exactly once
2. **Numbered for Order** — Read 00→10 sequentially or jump to specific topics
3. **Validated Links** — All internal references resolve correctly
4. **Current + Target** — Documents distinguish implemented vs planned features
```

### 2. Create Numbered Documentation Files

#### docs/00-INTRODUCTION.md (~50 lines)
- Project name, vision, and goals
- Monorepo structure overview (apps/, packages/)
- Technology stack summary (Next.js, TypeScript, DDD, Clean Arch)
- Link to 01-GETTING-STARTED for setup

#### docs/01-GETTING-STARTED.md (~80 lines)
- Prerequisites (Node.js, pnpm)
- Clone, install, and run dev server
- Monorepo commands (`pnpm dev`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm test`)
- Development workflow (Issue → Branch → PR protocol from CLAUDE.md)
- Task Master integration basics (link to .taskmaster/CLAUDE.md for details)

#### docs/02-ARCHITECTURE.md (~150 lines)
**Merge and deduplicate:**
- Current docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
- Relevant sections from CLAUDE.md
- References from packages/core/ARCHITECTURE.md (link, don't duplicate)

**Contents:**
- Clean Architecture diagram (core ← application ← infra ← web/api)
- Layer responsibilities and restrictions
- Dependency rule enforcement (ESLint patterns from CLAUDE.md)
- Monorepo build order
- Link to 03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS for DDD details

#### docs/03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md (~120 lines)
**Merge:**
- Current docs/BOUNDED_CONTEXTS.md
- DDD section from CLAUDE.md
- Shared Kernel definition

**Contents:**
- Bounded contexts diagram (Portfolio, Blog, Contact + Shared Kernel)
- Context responsibilities and boundaries
- Rules between contexts (no direct imports)
- Aggregate structure
- Link to packages/core/ARCHITECTURE.md for internal Core structure

#### docs/04-APPLICATION-LAYER.md (~100 lines)
**Source:** docs/APPLICATION.md (rename and refine)
- Use cases structure
- Ports (IProjectRepository, IEmailService, etc.)
- DTOs and data flow
- Link to 05-API-CONTRACTS for edge layer

#### docs/05-API-CONTRACTS.md (~120 lines)
**Merge:**
- docs/API.md
- docs/ERROR_HANDLING.md

**Contents:**
- API envelope format (success/error)
- HTTP error mapping (NotFoundError → 404, etc.)
- Error codes and i18n strategy
- Next.js API routes structure

#### docs/06-VALIDATION.md (~80 lines)
**Source:** docs/VALIDATION.md (keep as-is, minor cleanup)

#### docs/07-I18N.md (~70 lines)
**Source:** docs/I18N.md (keep as-is, minor cleanup)

#### docs/08-TESTING.md (~200 lines)
**Source:** docs/TESTING.md + testing section from CLAUDE.md
**Merge and unify:**
- Testing stack (Vitest, Jest, Playwright)
- TDD cycle (Red → Green → Refactor)
- What to test by layer
- Test naming conventions
- Builders and fixtures rules

#### docs/09-PATTERNS.md (~180 lines)
**Extract from CLAUDE.md:**
- DDD Implementation Patterns section (Either, VO, Entity, Repository templates)
- GoF Design Patterns section (Factory, Repository, Adapter, Strategy, Observer, Decorator, Builder)
- Code templates for each pattern
- When to apply patterns (only when solving real problems)

#### docs/10-GLOSSARY.md (~200 lines)
**Merge both GLOSSARY.md files:**
- packages/core/GLOSSARY.md (285 lines — domain-heavy)
- docs/GLOSSARY.md (126 lines — architectural terms)

**Structure:**
- Domain Terms (Profile, Project, Experience, Skill, etc.)
- Architectural Terms (Aggregate, Value Object, Entity, Repository, Port, Adapter, etc.)
- Process Terms (PRD, Task Master, Sprint, etc.)
- Alphabetical within each section

### 3. Update packages/core/README.md (~40 lines)

Replace packages/core/ARCHITECTURE.md reference with link to unified docs:

```markdown
# @repo/core — Domain Layer

> **Domain layer of the Portfolio monorepo.**  
> Contains entities, value objects, aggregates, and repository interfaces.

## Documentation

For complete architecture and DDD documentation, see:
- **[docs/INDEX.md](../../docs/INDEX.md)** — Documentation navigation
- **[docs/02-ARCHITECTURE.md](../../docs/02-ARCHITECTURE.md)** — Clean Architecture overview
- **[docs/03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md](../../docs/03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md)** — DDD contexts and boundaries
- **[docs/10-GLOSSARY.md](../../docs/10-GLOSSARY.md)** — Ubiquitous language

## This Package

`@repo/core` is the nucleus of the system with **zero external framework dependencies**.

**Forbidden imports:** React, Next.js, Prisma, Axios, HTTP libraries  
**Allowed:** Plain TypeScript, other @repo/core modules, @repo/utils, uuid

## Structure

```text
src/
  shared/           → Shared Kernel (Either, base errors, common VOs)
  portfolio/        → Portfolio context (Project, Experience, Profile)
  blog/             → Blog context (future)
  contact/          → Contact context (future)
```

See [decisions/](./decisions/) for Architectural Decision Records (ADRs).
```

### 4. Delete Duplicate Files

Remove these files after content is merged:
- `packages/core/ARCHITECTURE.md` (merged into docs/02-ARCHITECTURE.md + docs/03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md)
- `packages/core/GLOSSARY.md` (merged into docs/10-GLOSSARY.md)
- `docs/README.md` (replaced by docs/INDEX.md)
- `docs/APPLICATION.md` (renamed to docs/04-APPLICATION-LAYER.md)
- `docs/API.md` (merged into docs/05-API-CONTRACTS.md)
- `docs/ERROR_HANDLING.md` (merged into docs/05-API-CONTRACTS.md)
- `docs/BOUNDED_CONTEXTS.md` (merged into docs/03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md)
- `docs/000-template.md` (move to packages/core/decisions/adr-template.md if not already there, or delete if duplicate)

### 5. Slim Down CLAUDE.md to ~200 Lines

**Keep ONLY:**
1. Role and Identity section (~10 lines)
2. Monorepo Structure diagram (~15 lines)
3. Link to docs/INDEX.md for complete documentation (~10 lines)
4. Code templates (Either, VO, Entity, Repository — ~80 lines)
5. Development Workflow (Issue → Branch → PR protocol — ~30 lines)
6. Task Master integration import (~10 lines)
7. Essential rules (anti-patterns, dependency rule summary — ~30 lines)

**Remove (now in docs/):**
- Supporting Documentation list → replaced by link to docs/INDEX.md
- Clean Architecture full explanation → docs/02-ARCHITECTURE.md
- DDD full section → docs/03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md
- Next.js App Router details → docs/04-APPLICATION-LAYER.md or new 01-GETTING-STARTED.md
- TanStack Query, Zod, Tailwind, Turborepo, TypeScript config → docs/01-GETTING-STARTED.md or docs/02-ARCHITECTURE.md
- ESLint architecture protection → docs/02-ARCHITECTURE.md
- TDD / Testing full section → docs/08-TESTING.md
- Design Patterns section → docs/09-PATTERNS.md

**Target CLAUDE.md structure (~200 lines):**

```markdown
# Claude — Portfolio Monorepo

## 🧠 Role and Identity

You are a senior software engineer specialized in TypeScript, DDD, Clean Architecture, and the Next.js ecosystem.
Before writing any code, think about the architecture, applicable patterns, and tests.
Always prioritize clean, testable, extensible code aligned with the layers defined in the documentation.

## 📁 Monorepo Structure

```text
apps/
  web/          → Public portfolio (Next.js 14+ App Router)
  blog/         → Blog (Next.js — future, post-MVP)
  api/          → Backend (Next.js API Routes)

packages/
  core/         → Domain + Shared Kernel (entities, VOs, repository interfaces)
  application/  → Use Cases, DTOs, ports
  infra/        → Concrete repositories (Prisma + Supabase)
  ui/           → Shared design system
  markdown/     → MDX / Markdown parser
  i18n/         → Shared translations
```

## 📚 Complete Documentation

**All project documentation lives in `docs/` with a numbered, logical structure.**

Start here: **[docs/INDEX.md](./docs/INDEX.md)**

Quick links:
- New to the project? [docs/00-INTRODUCTION.md](./docs/00-INTRODUCTION.md)
- Architecture & DDD? [docs/02-ARCHITECTURE.md](./docs/02-ARCHITECTURE.md) + [docs/03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md](./docs/03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md)
- Testing strategy? [docs/08-TESTING.md](./docs/08-TESTING.md)
- Looking for a term? [docs/10-GLOSSARY.md](./docs/10-GLOSSARY.md)

## 🏛️ Core Principle: Dependency Rule

Dependencies point **only inward**:

```text
core ← application ← infra ← web/api
```

**packages/core**: zero framework dependencies (no React, Next.js, Prisma, Axios)  
**packages/application**: use cases + ports, depends only on core  
**packages/infra**: implements ports, knows core + application  
**apps/web, apps/api**: presentation, consumes application layer

See [docs/02-ARCHITECTURE.md](./docs/02-ARCHITECTURE.md) for complete layer rules.

## 🧩 DDD Code Templates

### Either Pattern (mandatory for domain errors)

```typescript
// packages/core/src/shared/either.ts
type Either<L, R> = Left<L, R> | Right<L, R>;
class Left<L, R> { constructor(readonly value: L) {} ... }
class Right<L, R> { constructor(readonly value: R) {} ... }
export const left = <L, R>(v: L): Either<L, R> => new Left(v);
export const right = <L, R>(v: R): Either<L, R> => new Right(v);
```

### Value Object Template

```typescript
class Slug {
  private constructor(private readonly value: string) {}

  static create(raw: string): Either<DomainError, Slug> {
    if (!raw?.trim() || raw.length < 3) return left(new InvalidSlugError());
    const slug = raw.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, '-');
    return right(new Slug(slug));
  }

  toString(): string { return this.value; }
  equals(other: Slug): boolean { return this.value === other.value; }
}
```

### Entity Template

```typescript
class Project {
  private constructor(
    public readonly id: ProjectId,
    private title: LocalizedText,
    private slug: Slug,
    private status: ProjectStatus,
  ) {}

  static create(props: CreateProjectProps): Either<DomainError, Project> {
    const slug = Slug.create(props.slug);
    if (slug.isLeft()) return left(slug.value);
    return right(new Project(...));
  }

  publish(): Either<DomainError, void> {
    if (this.status === ProjectStatus.PUBLISHED)
      return left(new ProjectAlreadyPublishedError());
    this.status = ProjectStatus.PUBLISHED;
    return right(undefined);
  }
}
```

### Repository Interface (in core)

```typescript
// packages/core/src/portfolio/repositories/IProjectRepository.ts
interface IProjectRepository {
  findAll(): Promise<Project[]>;
  findBySlug(slug: Slug): Promise<Project | null>;
  save(project: Project): Promise<void>;
}
```

## 🔄 Development Workflow

### Issue → Branch → PR Protocol

1. **Task Master tag**: ensure task exists under correct Sprint tag
2. **Verify work not done**: check issue status, merged PRs, commits, branches
3. **Set Task Master to In Progress**: `task-master set-status --id=<id> --status=in-progress`
4. **Move GitHub issue to "In Progress"**: update in all linked Project boards
5. **Create branch from issue**: `gh issue develop <issue-number> --checkout`
6. **Implement**: write code, tests, commits
7. **Open PR against `develop`**: `gh pr create --base develop`
8. **Set Task Master to Done**: `task-master set-status --id=<id> --status=done`

### Rules

- Task Master status + GitHub Projects status mirror reality at all times
- PRs always target `develop`, never `master` or `staging`
- Lock-file conflicts: `git checkout --theirs pnpm-lock.yaml && pnpm install`

## 🚫 Anti-Patterns

- Business logic in React components or repositories
- Import Prisma/ORM inside `core` or `application`
- Use `throw` for domain errors (use Either pattern)
- Public setters on entities (use business methods)
- `any` in types (use explicit types or `unknown`)
- Tests that verify implementation instead of behavior

## Task Master AI Integration

**Import Task Master's development workflow:**  
@./.taskmaster/CLAUDE.md
```

### 6. Validate All Internal Links

After creating all files, verify:

```bash
# Check all markdown links resolve
find docs -name "*.md" -exec grep -H "\[.*\](\..*\.md)" {} \; | while read line; do
  # Extract file path and link target, verify target exists
done

# Or use a markdown link checker tool
npx markdown-link-check docs/**/*.md
```

### 7. Update References in Code Comments

Search codebase for references to old doc paths and update:

```bash
# Find references to old docs
rg "docs/APPLICATION\.md" --type ts
rg "docs/ERROR_HANDLING\.md" --type ts
rg "packages/core/ARCHITECTURE\.md" --type ts

# Update to new paths:
# docs/APPLICATION.md → docs/04-APPLICATION-LAYER.md
# docs/ERROR_HANDLING.md → docs/05-API-CONTRACTS.md
# packages/core/ARCHITECTURE.md → docs/02-ARCHITECTURE.md or docs/03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md
```

---

## Deduplication Strategy

### GLOSSARY Merge (packages/core/GLOSSARY.md + docs/GLOSSARY.md → docs/10-GLOSSARY.md)

- **Domain terms** (Profile, Project, Experience, Skill, etc.) from packages/core/GLOSSARY.md
- **Architectural terms** (Aggregate, Entity, VO, Repository, Port, etc.) from docs/GLOSSARY.md
- **Process terms** (Sprint, Task Master, PRD, etc.) from context
- Remove duplicate definitions (e.g., both files define "Entity", keep one authoritative version)

### ARCHITECTURE Merge (docs/ARCHITECTURE.md + packages/core/ARCHITECTURE.md → docs/02-ARCHITECTURE.md + docs/03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md)

- **docs/02-ARCHITECTURE.md**: layer rules, dependency direction, Clean Arch principles
- **docs/03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md**: DDD contexts, aggregates, shared kernel, context boundaries
- **packages/core/README.md**: link to above, explain Core's specific role and structure

### API + ERROR_HANDLING Merge (docs/API.md + docs/ERROR_HANDLING.md → docs/05-API-CONTRACTS.md)

- Envelope format (success/error structure)
- Error codes and HTTP mapping
- i18n at the edge
- Next.js route structure

---

## Success Criteria

1. **Single source of truth**: each concept appears exactly once
2. **CLAUDE.md ~200 lines**: slimmed from 635 to ~200 by moving content to docs/
3. **Numbered structure**: docs/00-*.md through docs/10-*.md in logical reading order
4. **INDEX.md complete**: comprehensive navigation with all links tested
5. **All internal links resolve**: no broken references
6. **No duplicate files**: old ARCHITECTURE.md, GLOSSARY.md, APPLICATION.md, API.md, ERROR_HANDLING.md deleted
7. **Code comments updated**: any references to old doc paths updated to new numbered structure
tasks.testStrategy:
## Test Strategy

### 1. Link Validation

**Automated check:**
```bash
# Install markdown link checker
npm install -g markdown-link-check

# Check all internal links resolve
markdown-link-check docs/INDEX.md
markdown-link-check docs/*.md
markdown-link-check CLAUDE.md
markdown-link-check packages/core/README.md

# Or use find + grep to manually verify
find docs -name "*.md" -exec grep -H "\[.*\](\..*\.md)" {} \; > link-inventory.txt
# Manually verify each link in link-inventory.txt points to existing file
```

**Expected result:** All internal markdown links resolve to existing files.

### 2. Line Count Verification

```bash
# Verify CLAUDE.md is ~200 lines (target: 180-220)
wc -l CLAUDE.md

# Verify total docs line count is reasonable (target: ~1500-2000 lines total)
find docs -name "*.md" -exec wc -l {} + | tail -1

# Verify individual files are within expected ranges:
wc -l docs/00-INTRODUCTION.md      # ~50 lines
wc -l docs/01-GETTING-STARTED.md   # ~80 lines
wc -l docs/02-ARCHITECTURE.md      # ~150 lines
wc -l docs/03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md  # ~120 lines
wc -l docs/08-TESTING.md           # ~200 lines
wc -l docs/09-PATTERNS.md          # ~180 lines
wc -l docs/10-GLOSSARY.md          # ~200 lines
```

**Expected result:**

- docs/10-GLOSSARY.md: ~200 lines (merged from 285 + 126 = 411, deduplicated)
- Total docs/: reasonable consolidated length

### 3. Duplicate Content Detection

tasks.title: Implement Either.collect utility to eliminate repetitive Either propagation patterns
tasks.description: Create a Either.collect utility function in packages/core/src/shared/either.ts that accepts an array of Either<L,R> and returns Either<L, R[]> with the first error or all values, then refactor all entities and VOs to use it instead of repetitive if (result.isLeft()) return left(result.value) chains.

tasks.title: Enforce VO vs primitive+Validator rule on core entities
tasks.description: Audit all entities in packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/ and ensure every primitive or enum property has domain invariant validation in create() using Validator. Add missing validation for Project.status (ProjectStatus enum) and other primitives/enums without validation, following the rule: no new VOs for simple cases; use Validator.of(value).in([...]) or .refine() with a single left return.
tasks.details:
## Context

This task enforces the validation rule documented in `docs/06-VALIDATION.md`:
- **Every primitive or enum property** on an entity must have **domain invariant validation** in `create()` using `Validator`
- **Do not create new VOs** for simple cases (stable enums, booleans, primitives with trivial rules)
- Use `Validator.of(value).in([...])` for enums or `.refine()` for custom predicates
- Return `left(new ValidationError({ code, message }))` on failure

Gap identified: **Project.status** (ProjectStatus enum) has no validation in `Project.create()` at `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/project/model/Project.ts:101-185`.

---

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Audit All Entities for Missing Primitive/Enum Validation

Systematically review each entity's `create()` method in `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/` for properties that are:
- Primitives: `string`, `number`, `boolean`
- Enums: `ProjectStatus`, `SkillTypeValue`, `FluencyValue`, `EmploymentTypeValue`, `LocationTypeValue`
- NOT wrapped in a VO or validated with `Validator`

**Entities to audit:**

1. **Project** (`project/model/Project.ts`)
   - ✅ `slug` → wrapped in Slug VO
   - ✅ `coverImage` → wrapped in Image VO
   - ✅ `title`, `caption`, `summary`, `objectives`, `role`, `theme` → wrapped in LocalizedText VO
   - ✅ `content` → wrapped in Text VO
   - ✅ `skills` → array of Skill entities
   - ✅ `period` → wrapped in DateRange VO
   - ✅ `relatedProjects` → validated with Validator (lines 147-166)
   - ✅ `team` → primitive string (optional), no critical invariant
   - ❌ **`featured`** → boolean primitive, **NO validation** (line 96)
   - ❌ **`status`** → ProjectStatus enum, **NO validation** (line 97)

2. **Experience** (`experience/model/Experience.ts`)
   - ✅ All properties wrapped in VOs or validated (company, position, location, description → LocalizedText; logo → Image; employment_type, location_type → VOs; period → DateRange; skills → ExperienceSkill array)

3. **Skill** (`skill/model/Skill.ts`)
   - ✅ `description`, `icon` → wrapped in Text VO
   - ✅ `type` → wrapped in SkillType VO (validates SkillTypeValue enum)

4. **Language** (`language/model/Language.ts`)
   - ✅ `name` → wrapped in Name VO
   - ✅ `fluency` → wrapped in Fluency VO (validates FluencyValue enum)
   - ✅ `locale` → validated with Validator (lines 56-71)

5. **Profile** (`profile/model/Profile.ts`)
   - ✅ `name` → wrapped in Name VO
   - ✅ `headline`, `bio` → wrapped in LocalizedText VO
   - ✅ `photo` → wrapped in Image VO
   - ✅ `stats` → array of ProfileStat (validated)
   - ✅ `featuredProjectSlugs` → validated with Validator (lines 60-70)

6. **ProfileStat** (`profile/model/ProfileStat.ts`)
   - ✅ `label` → wrapped in LocalizedText VO
   - ✅ `icon` → wrapped in Text VO
   - ❌ **`value`** → primitive string, **NO validation** (line 26, assigned as `value.value`)
   - **Note:** ProfileStat.value is wrapped in Text VO on line 36, but then unwrapped to primitive string in constructor (line 42: `new ProfileStat(label, value.value, icon)`). This is a special case where the VO is used for validation but not retained.

7. **SocialNetwork** (`social-network/model/SocialNetwork.ts`)
   - ✅ `name` → wrapped in Name VO
   - ✅ `icon` → wrapped in Text VO
   - ✅ `url` → wrapped in Url VO

8. **ProfessionalValue** (`professional-value/model/ProfessionalValue.ts`)
   - ✅ `icon`, `content` → wrapped in Text VO

9. **ExperienceSkill** (`experience/model/ExperienceSkill.ts`)
   - ✅ `skill` → wrapped in Skill entity
   - ✅ `workDescription` → wrapped in LocalizedText VO

**Audit Result:**
- **Project.featured** (boolean) — missing validation
- **Project.status** (ProjectStatus enum) — missing validation
- **ProfileStat.value** (string) — validated via Text VO, then unwrapped; special case, acceptable

---

### 2. Add Validation for Project.status

**File:** `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/project/model/Project.ts`

**Location:** In `Project.create()`, after line 123 (after `fieldsResult` validation), add:

```typescript
// Validate status enum
const { error: statusError, isValid: statusValid } = Validator.of(props.status)
  .in(
    Object.values(ProjectStatus),
    `Status must be one of: ${Object.values(ProjectStatus).join(', ')}.`
  )
  .validate();

if (!statusValid && statusError)
  return left(
    new ValidationError({
      code: Project.ERROR_CODE,
      message: statusError,
    }),
  );
```

**Rationale:**
- `ProjectStatus` is a stable enum with 3 values (DRAFT, PUBLISHED, ARCHIVED)
- No need for a new VO wrapper — use `Validator.of(value).in([...])` directly
- Validates the enum value before entity construction
- Consistent with existing validation patterns (see Language.locale lines 56-71, Project.relatedProjects lines 147-166)

---

### 3. Add Validation for Project.featured

**File:** `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/project/model/Project.ts`

**Location:** After the status validation added in step 2, add:

```typescript
// Validate featured boolean
const { error: featuredError, isValid: featuredValid } = Validator.of(props.featured)
  .refine(
    (v) => typeof v === 'boolean',
    'Featured must be a boolean value.'
  )
  .validate();

if (!featuredValid && featuredError)
  return left(
    new ValidationError({
      code: Project.ERROR_CODE,
      message: featuredError,
    }),
  );
```

**Rationale:**
- `featured` is a boolean primitive — no need for a new VO
- At runtime, TypeScript types are not enforced; external data might pass invalid values
- Domain must validate its invariants: featured must be a boolean
- Uses `.refine()` with a type guard predicate

---

### 4. Verify ProfileStat.value Validation (No Changes Needed)

**File:** `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/profile/model/ProfileStat.ts`

**Analysis:**
- Line 36: `Text.create(props.value, { min: 1, max: 50 })` — validates value is a string with length constraints
- Line 42: `new ProfileStat(label, value.value, icon)` — unwraps Text VO to primitive string after validation
- **Conclusion:** ProfileStat.value is validated via Text VO; the unwrapping is intentional (ProfileStat is not an Entity, it's a simple data holder). **No changes needed.**

---

## Implementation Summary

**Files to modify:**
1. `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/project/model/Project.ts`
   - Add validation for `props.status` (enum)
   - Add validation for `props.featured` (boolean)

**Validation placement:** Insert both validations **after** line 123 (after `fieldsResult` check) and **before** line 139 (before relatedProjects validation).

**Expected outcome:**
- All entities have domain invariant validation for primitive/enum properties
- No new VOs created (following the rule: use Validator for simple cases)
- All existing tests continue to pass (validation only rejects invalid inputs that would fail at runtime anyway)

---

## Edge Cases & Considerations

1. **Optional primitives:** `Project.team` (string | undefined) — no critical invariant, left as-is (assigned on line 94 without validation). If a constraint is needed (e.g., max length), add `Validator.of(props.team).length(1, 200, 'Team must be...').validate()` with optional handling.

2. **Enum validation pattern:** Use `Object.values(EnumName)` for `.in()` to ensure all enum values are accepted.

3. **Boolean validation:** Use `.refine((v) => typeof v === 'boolean', 'Message')` to guard against non-boolean values at runtime.

4. **Error codes:** Use the entity's existing `ERROR_CODE` constant (`Project.ERROR_CODE`) for all validation errors.

5. **Test impact:** Existing tests should pass — validation only rejects invalid inputs (non-booleans, invalid enum values) that tests do not provide.

---

## References

- **Validation strategy:** `docs/06-VALIDATION.md`
- **Validator API:** `packages/utils/src/validator/Validator.ts`
- **Existing validation patterns:**
  - Language.locale: `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/language/model/Language.ts:56-71`
  - Project.relatedProjects: `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/project/model/Project.ts:147-166`
  - SkillType, Fluency, EmploymentType, LocationType VOs: `packages/core/src/shared/vo/`

**Expected:** All documented examples should match the actual implementation.

### 7. Regression Test — Existing Sections

tasks.title: Update docs/ to reflect VO vs primitive+Validator rule
tasks.description: Update docs/09-PATTERNS.md and docs/06-VALIDATION.md to document the architectural rule for when to create Value Objects versus when to use primitives/enums with Validator, ensuring consistency with CLAUDE.md guidance.
tasks.details:
## Overview

Task 15 will document the VO vs primitive+Validator rule in CLAUDE.md as the primary reference. This task ensures that the formal documentation in `docs/` reinforces this rule through concise cross-references, without duplicating content.

## Current State Analysis

**docs/09-PATTERNS.md:**
- Contains comprehensive templates for Either, Value Object, Entity, and Repository patterns
- Entity template (lines 126-165) shows composition with VOs but doesn't explain when to use VOs vs primitives
- No guidance on the decision criteria for creating a VO vs validating a primitive/enum directly

**docs/06-VALIDATION.md:**
- Documents where validation happens (API/Forms use Zod, Domain uses Validator)
- Shows VO validation example (Slug) but doesn't mention when primitives/enums should be validated directly
- Missing guidance that stable enums like ProjectStatus don't require VOs

**Codebase examples:**
- `ProjectStatus` (enum): Used directly in Project entity without a VO wrapper
- `SkillType` (enum): Wrapped in a VO with Validator.in() validation
- `featured` (boolean): Used as primitive in Project entity
- `team` (string): Used as optional primitive in Project entity
- `relatedProjects`: Validated as array of Slugs using Validator.refine() in Project.create()

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Update docs/09-PATTERNS.md

**Location:** After the Entity Template section (after line 165), before "Repository Interface Template"

**Add new subsection:**

```markdown
### Entity Properties: VO vs Primitive + Validator

**When to create a Value Object:**
- Concept has rich domain semantics (Slug, Email, DateRange)
- Needs multiple validation rules or behavior methods
- Reused across multiple entities or contexts
- Type represents a domain concept, not just a data type

**When to use primitive/enum + Validator:**
- Stable enum with fixed, well-known values (ProjectStatus, Locale)
- Simple boolean flag with domain invariant validation
- Single validation rule (e.g., string length, numeric range)
- Property is entity-specific and unlikely to be extracted

**Example — primitive enum validated in create():**
```typescript
// ProjectStatus is a stable enum — no VO needed
export enum ProjectStatus { DRAFT = 'DRAFT', PUBLISHED = 'PUBLISHED', ARCHIVED = 'ARCHIVED' }

class Project extends Entity<Project, IProjectProps> {
  public readonly status: ProjectStatus;

  static create(props: IProjectProps): Either<ValidationError, Project> {
    const { error, isValid } = Validator.of(props.status)
      .in([ProjectStatus.DRAFT, ProjectStatus.PUBLISHED, ProjectStatus.ARCHIVED], 'Invalid status')
      .validate();
    if (!isValid && error)
      return left(new ValidationError({ code: Project.ERROR_CODE, message: error }));
    // ... rest of entity creation
  }
}
```

See [CLAUDE.md § Domain Validation](../CLAUDE.md#domain-validation-core) for full guidance and examples.
```

### 2. Update docs/06-VALIDATION.md

**Location:** In the "Domain Invariants" section (after line 61), before the Validator methods table

**Add paragraph:**

```markdown
**Primitives and enums in entities:**

For stable enums (e.g., `ProjectStatus`) or simple boolean/string properties with single invariants, validate directly in `Entity.create()` using `Validator` — a dedicated Value Object is not required. Reserve VOs for rich domain concepts with multiple rules or reusable semantics.

```typescript
// ✅ Enum validated directly in create()
static create(props: IProjectProps): Either<ValidationError, Project> {
  const { error, isValid } = Validator.of(props.status)
    .in([ProjectStatus.DRAFT, ProjectStatus.PUBLISHED], 'Invalid status')
    .validate();
  if (!isValid && error)
    return left(new ValidationError({ code: Project.ERROR_CODE, message: error }));
  // ...
}
```

For guidance on VO vs primitive decisions, see [09-PATTERNS § Entity Properties](./09-PATTERNS.md#entity-properties-vo-vs-primitive--validator).
```

### 3. Verification

After implementation:
1. Read both files to verify additions
2. Check that cross-references point to correct sections
3. Ensure no duplication of CLAUDE.md content — only reinforcement and cross-reference
4. Verify markdown formatting and heading hierarchy

## Files Modified

- `docs/09-PATTERNS.md` — add subsection "Entity Properties: VO vs Primitive + Validator"
- `docs/06-VALIDATION.md` — add paragraph in "Domain Invariants" section

## Key Principles

- **Concise additions:** 1-2 paragraphs per file, no lengthy explanations
- **Cross-reference CLAUDE.md:** Point to CLAUDE.md as single source of truth
- **No duplication:** Don't repeat examples already in CLAUDE.md
- **Reinforce architecture:** Make the rule discoverable to any engineer reading docs/
tasks.testStrategy:

grep -A 30 "Entity Properties: VO vs Primitive" docs/09-PATTERNS.md

**Verify:**

tasks.title: Configure packages/infra with Prisma + Supabase
tasks.description: Set up the infrastructure package with Prisma ORM connected to Supabase Postgres, including complete schema definition and initial migration.
tasks.details:
1. Initialize packages/infra with package.json and tsconfig.json
2. Install dependencies: prisma, @prisma/client
3. Create schema.prisma with datasource db (provider: postgresql, url from env DATABASE_URL, directUrl from env DIRECT_URL)
4. Define models following the domain entities:
   - Project: id (uuid @id @default(uuid())), slug (unique), coverImageUrl, coverImageAlt (jsonb for LocalizedText), title (jsonb), caption (jsonb), content (text), skills (relation), theme (jsonb optional), summary (jsonb optional), objectives (jsonb optional), role (jsonb optional), team (string optional), periodStart (datetime), periodEnd (datetime optional), featured (boolean), status (enum: DRAFT, PUBLISHED, ARCHIVED), relatedProjectSlugs (string[]), createdAt, updatedAt, deletedAt (optional)
   - Skill: id (uuid), description (jsonb), icon (string), type (enum: TECHNICAL, SOFT, LANGUAGE, TOOL)
   - Experience: id (uuid), company (jsonb), position (jsonb), location (jsonb), description (jsonb), logoUrl, logoAlt (jsonb), employmentType (enum), locationType (enum), startAt (datetime), endAt (datetime optional), createdAt, updatedAt
   - ExperienceSkill: experienceId, skillId, workDescription (jsonb), @@id([experienceId, skillId])
   - Profile: id (uuid), name (string), headline (jsonb), bio (jsonb), photoUrl, photoAlt (jsonb), featuredProjectSlugs (string[])
   - ProfileStat: id (uuid), profileId, label (jsonb), value (string), order (int)
   - SocialNetwork: id (uuid), profileId, name (string), url (string), icon (string)
5. Create src/prisma/client.ts with singleton PrismaClient export
6. Add scripts to package.json: db:generate (prisma generate), db:migrate (prisma migrate dev), db:studio (prisma studio)
7. Run prisma migrate dev --name init to create first migration
8. Update .env.example with DATABASE_URL and DIRECT_URL placeholders
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify prisma generate succeeds without errors
2. Verify migration applies cleanly on fresh database
3. Verify PrismaClient singleton can be imported and instantiated
4. Run prisma studio to visually inspect schema
5. Test connection with simple query: prisma.project.findMany()

subtasks.title: Initialize packages/infra with package.json and TypeScript config
subtasks.description: Set up the infrastructure package foundation with proper package.json configuration, TypeScript config, and folder structure.
subtasks.details:
1. Create packages/infra directory structure with src/ folder
2. Initialize package.json with name '@repo/infra', version, and proper exports
3. Create tsconfig.json extending base config with strict mode and proper paths
4. Add .gitignore for node_modules, dist, .env
5. Install core dependencies: prisma (dev), @prisma/client, and typescript tooling
6. Set up src/index.ts as main entry point exporting repositories
7. Verify package builds with tsc --noEmit
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Run pnpm install to verify dependencies resolve
2. Run tsc --noEmit to verify TypeScript config is valid
3. Verify package can be imported from apps/web with proper module resolution
4. Check package.json exports field resolves correctly

subtasks.title: Create schema.prisma datasource configuration and Project model
subtasks.description: Set up Prisma schema file with PostgreSQL datasource pointing to Supabase and define the Project model with all JSONB fields, relations, and enums.
subtasks.details:
1. Create prisma/schema.prisma with generator client and datasource db (provider: postgresql, url: env('DATABASE_URL'), directUrl: env('DIRECT_URL'))
2. Define ProjectStatus enum (DRAFT, PUBLISHED, ARCHIVED)
3. Define Project model with:
   - id: String @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
   - slug: String @unique
   - coverImageUrl: String, coverImageAlt: Json (for LocalizedText)
   - title: Json, caption: Json, summary: Json?, objectives: Json?, role: Json?, theme: Json?
   - content: String @db.Text
   - team: String?
   - periodStart: DateTime, periodEnd: DateTime?
   - featured: Boolean @default(false)
   - status: ProjectStatus @default(DRAFT)
   - relatedProjectSlugs: String[]
   - createdAt: DateTime @default(now()), updatedAt: DateTime @updatedAt, deletedAt: DateTime?
   - skills relation to Skill[] via ProjectSkill join table
4. Update .env.example with DATABASE_URL and DIRECT_URL placeholders
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Run prisma format to validate schema syntax
2. Run prisma validate to check for errors
3. Verify DATABASE_URL connection with prisma db pull (should show empty schema)
4. Check that JSONB fields are properly typed as Json in schema

subtasks.title: Define Skill model and ProjectSkill join table
subtasks.description: Create the Skill model with type enum and the many-to-many ProjectSkill join table connecting projects to skills.
subtasks.details:
1. Define SkillType enum (TECHNICAL, SOFT, LANGUAGE, TOOL)
2. Define Skill model:
   - id: String @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
   - description: Json (for LocalizedText)
   - icon: String
   - type: SkillType
   - projects: Project[] relation via ProjectSkill
   - experiences: ExperienceSkill[]
   - createdAt: DateTime @default(now()), updatedAt: DateTime @updatedAt
3. Define ProjectSkill join table:
   - projectId: String @db.Uuid
   - skillId: String @db.Uuid
   - project: Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
   - skill: Skill @relation(fields: [skillId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
   - @@id([projectId, skillId])
   - @@index([skillId])
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Run prisma format and validate
2. Check relation syntax for many-to-many is correct
3. Verify cascade delete is configured properly
4. Generate types with prisma generate and inspect ProjectSkill type definition

subtasks.title: Define Experience and ExperienceSkill models with relations
subtasks.description: Create Experience model with JSONB localized fields and ExperienceSkill join table that includes workDescription.
subtasks.details:
1. Define EmploymentType enum (FULL_TIME, PART_TIME, CONTRACT, INTERNSHIP, FREELANCE)
2. Define LocationType enum (ONSITE, REMOTE, HYBRID)
3. Define Experience model:
   - id: String @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
   - company: Json, position: Json, location: Json, description: Json (all LocalizedText)
   - logoUrl: String, logoAlt: Json
   - employmentType: EmploymentType
   - locationType: LocationType
   - startAt: DateTime, endAt: DateTime?
   - createdAt: DateTime @default(now()), updatedAt: DateTime @updatedAt
   - skills: ExperienceSkill[]
4. Define ExperienceSkill model:
   - experienceId: String @db.Uuid
   - skillId: String @db.Uuid
   - workDescription: Json (LocalizedText explaining how skill was used)
   - experience: Experience @relation(fields: [experienceId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
   - skill: Skill @relation(fields: [skillId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
   - @@id([experienceId, skillId])
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Run prisma format and validate
2. Verify ExperienceSkill has workDescription field (not just a join table)
3. Check cascade delete on both relations
4. Generate and inspect types to ensure workDescription is included

subtasks.title: Define Profile, ProfileStat, and SocialNetwork models
subtasks.description: Create Profile model with one-to-many relations to ProfileStat and SocialNetwork for portfolio metadata.
subtasks.details:
1. Define Profile model:
   - id: String @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
   - name: String
   - headline: Json, bio: Json (LocalizedText)
   - photoUrl: String, photoAlt: Json
   - featuredProjectSlugs: String[] (max 6, validated in domain)
   - stats: ProfileStat[]
   - socialNetworks: SocialNetwork[]
   - createdAt: DateTime @default(now()), updatedAt: DateTime @updatedAt
2. Define ProfileStat model:
   - id: String @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
   - profileId: String @db.Uuid
   - label: Json (LocalizedText)
   - value: String
   - order: Int
   - profile: Profile @relation(fields: [profileId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
   - @@index([profileId, order])
3. Define SocialNetwork model:
   - id: String @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
   - profileId: String @db.Uuid
   - name: String
   - url: String
   - icon: String
   - profile: Profile @relation(fields: [profileId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
   - @@index([profileId])
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Run prisma format and validate
2. Verify one-to-many relations are correctly defined
3. Check indexes on profileId for efficient queries
4. Generate types and verify ProfileStat has order field for sorting

subtasks.title: Create PrismaClient singleton and package scripts
subtasks.description: Set up reusable PrismaClient singleton with proper connection handling and add npm scripts for database operations.
subtasks.details:
1. Create src/prisma/client.ts:
   - Import PrismaClient from @prisma/client
   - Implement singleton pattern with globalThis caching (prevent multiple instances in dev)
   - Export const prisma = globalThis.prisma || new PrismaClient({ log: ['query', 'error', 'warn'] })
   - In dev mode: globalThis.prisma = prisma
   - Add graceful shutdown: process.on('beforeExit', () => prisma.$disconnect())
2. Add scripts to package.json:
   - "db:generate": "prisma generate"
   - "db:migrate": "prisma migrate dev"
   - "db:migrate:deploy": "prisma migrate deploy" (for production)
   - "db:studio": "prisma studio"
   - "db:push": "prisma db push" (for dev quick sync)
   - "db:seed": "tsx prisma/seed.ts" (optional, for later)
3. Export prisma client from src/index.ts
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Import prisma client in a test file and verify it instantiates
2. Run db:generate and check .prisma/client is created
3. Test connection with prisma.$queryRaw`SELECT 1` in a simple script
4. Verify singleton behavior by importing twice and checking same instance
5. Check graceful shutdown hook is registered

subtasks.title: Generate and apply initial database migration
subtasks.description: Create the first Prisma migration that applies the complete schema to Supabase Postgres and verify it succeeds.
subtasks.details:
1. Ensure .env has valid DATABASE_URL and DIRECT_URL pointing to Supabase
2. Run prisma migrate dev --name init to:
   - Generate SQL migration in prisma/migrations/
   - Apply migration to database
   - Generate Prisma Client types
3. Review generated SQL migration file to ensure:
   - All tables created with correct columns
   - JSONB columns use jsonb type
   - UUID columns use uuid type with gen_random_uuid() default
   - Indexes created on foreign keys and slug
   - Cascade deletes configured
   - Enums created as PostgreSQL enums
4. Run prisma studio to visually inspect schema
5. Commit migration files to git
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify migration SQL file exists in prisma/migrations/
2. Check database with prisma studio shows all 8 tables
3. Run prisma db pull to reverse-engineer and compare with schema.prisma
4. Execute test query: await prisma.project.findMany() (should return [])
5. Test cascade delete by creating related records and deleting parent
6. Verify UUID defaults work by creating record without id

subtasks.title: Test complete schema with seed data and queries
subtasks.description: Validate the entire Prisma schema by creating comprehensive seed data covering all models and relations, then testing queries.
subtasks.details:
1. Create prisma/seed.ts with:
   - Sample Project with JSONB LocalizedText fields (en, es)
   - Sample Skills (3-4) with different types
   - ProjectSkill relations linking project to skills
   - Sample Experience with ExperienceSkill including workDescription
   - Sample Profile with ProfileStats (ordered) and SocialNetworks
   - Use realistic data matching domain constraints
2. Add seed script: "db:seed": "tsx prisma/seed.ts"
3. Run seed and verify data appears in Prisma Studio
4. Test queries:
   - prisma.project.findMany({ include: { skills: { include: { skill: true } } } })
   - prisma.experience.findMany({ include: { skills: { include: { skill: true } } }, orderBy: { startAt: 'desc' } })
   - prisma.profile.findFirst({ include: { stats: { orderBy: { order: 'asc' } }, socialNetworks: true } })
5. Verify JSONB fields parse correctly and UUIDs are valid
subtasks.testStrategy:
1. Run db:seed and verify no errors
2. Check Prisma Studio shows all seeded data with correct relations
3. Write simple test script that queries all models and logs results
4. Verify JSONB fields return proper objects (not strings)
5. Test filtering: prisma.project.findMany({ where: { status: 'PUBLISHED', featured: true } })
6. Clean up: prisma migrate reset to test migration + seed workflow

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down the Prisma + Supabase infrastructure setup into subtasks covering: (1) package initialization with dependencies, (2) schema.prisma datasource configuration, (3) Project model definition with JSONB fields, (4) Experience + ExperienceSkill models with relations, (5) Profile + ProfileStat + SocialNetwork models, (6) Skill model, (7) PrismaClient singleton setup, (8) initial migration generation and testing. Each subtask should be independently testable.

tasks.title: Implement PrismaProjectRepository
tasks.description: Create concrete implementation of IProjectRepository using Prisma, including mapper from Prisma model to domain Project entity.
tasks.details:
1. Create ProjectMapper.ts with methods:
   - toDomain(prismaProject): Project - converts Prisma result to domain entity
     - Parse JSONB fields to LocalizedText VOs
     - Parse slug to Slug VO
     - Parse coverImage to Image VO
     - Parse periodStart/End to DateRange VO
     - Parse skills relation to Skill[] entities
     - Parse relatedProjectSlugs to Slug[] VOs
     - Use Project.create() to validate and instantiate
   - toPrisma(project): Prisma.ProjectCreateInput - converts domain to Prisma input
2. Implement PrismaProjectRepository implementing IProjectRepository:
   - findAll(): Promise<Project[]> - prisma.project.findMany() with include skills, map with ProjectMapper
   - findPublished(): Promise<Project[]> - where: { status: PUBLISHED, deletedAt: null }
   - findFeatured(): Promise<Project[]> - where: { featured: true, status: PUBLISHED, deletedAt: null }
   - findById(id: Id): Promise<Project | null> - prisma.project.findUnique({ where: { id: id.value } })
   - findBySlug(slug: Slug): Promise<Project | null> - prisma.project.findUnique({ where: { slug: slug.value } })
   - findRelated(id: Id, limit = 3): Promise<Project[]> - fetch project, then query where slug in relatedProjectSlugs and id != current, limit
   - save(project): Promise<void> - upsert logic
   - delete(id): Promise<void> - soft delete (set deletedAt)
3. Handle Either errors from domain mappers appropriately (throw infrastructure exceptions if mapping fails)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for ProjectMapper with mock Prisma data
2. Integration tests with test database:
   - Create project via save, verify findById returns same
   - Test findPublished filters correctly
   - Test findFeatured with featured=true projects
   - Test findRelated returns correct related projects
   - Test soft delete sets deletedAt
3. Test error handling when Prisma query fails
4. Test mapping errors propagate correctly

subtasks.title: Implement findAll, findPublished, and findFeatured query methods
subtasks.description: Implement repository read methods for listing projects with status filtering and soft-delete checks
subtasks.details:

   - First fetch current project: prisma.project.findUnique({ where: { id: id.value } })
   - If project is null or relatedProjectSlugs is empty, return empty array
   - Query related projects: prisma.project.findMany({
       where: {
         slug: { in: project.relatedProjectSlugs },
         id: { not: id.value },
         status: 'PUBLISHED',
         deletedAt: null
       },
       include: { skills: true },
       take: limit
     })
   - Map results using ProjectMapper.toDomain
   - Return array of related Project entities

subtasks.title: Implement save (upsert) and delete (soft-delete) operations
subtasks.description: Implement repository write methods for creating/updating projects and soft-deleting them
subtasks.details:
In packages/infra/src/repositories/project/PrismaProjectRepository.ts implement:
1. save(project: Project): Promise<void>
   - Convert project using ProjectMapper.toPrisma
   - Use prisma.project.upsert({
       where: { id: project.id?.value ?? 'new-id' },
       create: prismaData,
       update: prismaData
     })
   - Handle skills relation update (disconnect old, connect new)
   - Wrap in transaction if needed for atomicity
   - Return void on success, throw InfrastructureError on failure
2. delete(id: Id): Promise<void>
   - Soft delete: prisma.project.update({ where: { id: id.value }, data: { deletedAt: new Date() } })
   - Do NOT use prisma.project.delete (hard delete)
   - Return void on success
   - If project not found, throw InfrastructureError
3. Handle Prisma unique constraint violations appropriately

tasks.title: Implement ResendEmailService
tasks.description: Create concrete implementation of IEmailService using Resend API for sending contact form emails.
tasks.details:
1. Install dependency: resend
2. Create ResendEmailService.ts implementing IEmailService:
   - Constructor accepts Resend API client (injected)
   - send(message: IContactMessageDTO): Promise<Either<DomainError, void>>
     - Use resend.emails.send({
         from: 'onboarding@resend.dev' (or configured sender),
         to: process.env.CONTACT_EMAIL_TO,
         replyTo: message.email,
         subject: `Contact from ${message.name}`,
         html: template with message.name, message.email, message.message
       })
     - Wrap in try/catch
     - On success: return right(undefined)
     - On error: return left(new DomainError({ code: 'EMAIL_SEND_FAILED', message: error.message }))

4. Update .env.example with RESEND_API_KEY and CONTACT_EMAIL_TO
