tasks.title: Define Response Envelope and implement GET /api/v1/projects/:slug
tasks.description: Introduce consistent REST response envelope and implement the first API endpoint with thin handler that calls use cases and maps errors to HTTP.
tasks.details:
1. Create apps/web/src/lib/api/envelope.ts:
   - Define ApiResponse<T> type:
     - Success: { data: T, error: null, meta?: { timestamp: string, ... } }
     - Error: { data: null, error: { code: string, message: string, details?: unknown }, meta?: { ... } }
   - Helper functions:
     - successResponse<T>(data: T, meta?): ApiResponse<T>
     - errorResponse(code, message, details?, meta?): ApiResponse<never>
2. Create apps/web/src/lib/api/error-mapper.ts:
   - mapDomainErrorToHttp(error: DomainError, locale: string): { status: number, response: ApiResponse<never> }
     - NotFoundError → 404
     - ValidationError → 400
     - Generic DomainError → 400
     - Unknown error → 500
   - Localize error messages based on locale (use i18n if available, or default English)
3. Implement apps/web/src/app/api/v1/projects/[slug]/route.ts:
   - export async GET(request: NextRequest, { params }: { params: { slug: string } }):
     - Extract locale from request headers (Accept-Language) or use default
     - Get container from makeContainer()
     - Create GetProjectBySlug use case: new GetProjectBySlug(container.projectRepository)
     - Create Slug VO from params.slug
     - Execute use case: const result = await useCase.execute(slug)
     - If result.isLeft(): map error with mapDomainErrorToHttp, return NextResponse.json(response, { status })
     - If result.isRight(): return NextResponse.json(successResponse(result.value.toDTO()), { status: 200 })
   - No business logic in handler - only orchestration
4. Set CORS headers if needed
5. Add Next.js route config: export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic' (or 'auto' depending on caching strategy)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for envelope helpers
2. Unit tests for error mapper (each error type → correct status)
3. Integration tests for route:
   - Test with valid slug returns 200 + project data
   - Test with invalid slug returns 400 + validation error
   - Test with non-existent slug returns 404 + not found error
   - Test error localization works
4. Manual test with curl/Postman
5. Test response structure matches envelope format

subtasks.title: Create apps/web/src/lib/api/envelope.ts with ApiResponse type and helpers
subtasks.description: Define the ApiResponse<T> discriminated union type with success and error variants, including optional meta field for timestamp and other metadata. Implement successResponse and errorResponse helper functions.
subtasks.details:
Create envelope.ts in apps/web/src/lib/api/ directory. Define ApiResponse<T> as a discriminated union:
- Success variant: { data: T, error: null, meta?: { timestamp: string, [key: string]: unknown } }
- Error variant: { data: null, error: { code: string, message: string, details?: unknown }, meta?: { timestamp: string, [key: string]: unknown } }

Implement helper functions:
- successResponse<T>(data: T, meta?: Record<string, unknown>): ApiResponse<T> - constructs success response with auto-generated timestamp
- errorResponse(code: string, message: string, details?: unknown, meta?: Record<string, unknown>): ApiResponse<never> - constructs error response with auto-generated timestamp

Ensure type safety and proper generics usage. Follow TypeScript strict mode.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests verifying: (1) successResponse returns correct shape with data and null error, (2) errorResponse returns correct shape with null data and error object, (3) timestamp is automatically added to meta, (4) custom meta fields are preserved, (5) TypeScript type checking enforces discriminated union correctly

subtasks.title: Create apps/web/src/lib/api/error-mapper.ts with domain-to-HTTP error mapping
subtasks.description: Implement mapDomainErrorToHttp function that converts DomainError subclasses (ValidationError, NotFoundError, generic DomainError) to appropriate HTTP status codes and localized ApiResponse error objects.
subtasks.details:
Create error-mapper.ts in apps/web/src/lib/api/ directory. Implement:
- mapDomainErrorToHttp(error: DomainError, locale: string = 'en'): { status: number, response: ApiResponse<never> }

Mapping rules:
- NotFoundError → status 404
- ValidationError → status 400
- Generic DomainError → status 400
- Unknown/unexpected errors → status 500 with generic message

Localize error messages based on locale parameter. If i18n package is available in @repo/i18n, use it; otherwise fallback to English. Extract error.code and error.message from DomainError, pass error.details if present. Use errorResponse helper from envelope.ts to construct the response object.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for each error type: (1) ValidationError maps to 400 with correct code/message, (2) NotFoundError maps to 404, (3) Generic DomainError maps to 400, (4) Unknown error maps to 500, (5) Locale parameter affects message text, (6) Error details are included when present

subtasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/projects/[slug]/route.ts with slug extraction and locale handling
subtasks.description: Create Next.js App Router API route handler that extracts slug parameter from route params and locale from Accept-Language header, preparing inputs for use case execution.
subtasks.details:
Create route.ts in apps/web/src/app/api/v1/projects/[slug]/ directory. Implement:
- export async function GET(request: NextRequest, { params }: { params: { slug: string } }): Promise<NextResponse>

Extract slug from params.slug. Extract locale from request.headers.get('Accept-Language') or default to 'en'. Add route configuration:
- export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic' (or 'auto' based on caching strategy)

Set CORS headers if needed using NextResponse headers. Structure handler for orchestration only - no business logic. Import necessary Next.js types (NextRequest, NextResponse). Follow Next.js 14+ App Router conventions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests: (1) Route responds to GET requests, (2) Slug parameter is correctly extracted from URL, (3) Accept-Language header is parsed correctly, (4) Default locale is used when header absent, (5) CORS headers are present if configured, (6) Route config exports are correct

subtasks.title: Wire GetProjectBySlug use case from container and execute with Slug VO
subtasks.description: Instantiate GetProjectBySlug use case using dependency injection container, create Slug value object from route parameter, and execute the use case to retrieve project data.
subtasks.details:
In the route handler:
1. Get DI container: const container = makeContainer() (import from apps/web/src/lib/di or equivalent)
2. Get repository from container: const projectRepository = container.projectRepository
3. Instantiate use case: const getProjectBySlug = new GetProjectBySlug(projectRepository)
4. Create Slug VO from params.slug: const slugResult = Slug.create(params.slug)
5. Handle Slug creation failure: if (slugResult.isLeft()), map validation error to HTTP response early
6. Execute use case: const result = await getProjectBySlug.execute(slugResult.value)

Ensure proper import paths for GetProjectBySlug use case (from @repo/application), Slug VO (from @repo/core), and container (from apps/web). Follow dependency injection and Either pattern conventions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests: (1) Container provides correct repository instance, (2) Use case is instantiated with repository, (3) Invalid slug format returns 400 before use case execution, (4) Valid slug executes use case successfully, (5) Use case result is Either type, (6) DI container is properly imported and functioning

subtasks.title: Map Either result to HTTP response using envelope helpers
subtasks.description: Handle the Either result from GetProjectBySlug use case: on left (error), use mapDomainErrorToHttp and return error response; on right (success), convert entity to DTO and return success response.
subtasks.details:
In the route handler, after executing use case:
1. Check if result.isLeft():
   - Extract error: const error = result.value
   - Map to HTTP: const { status, response } = mapDomainErrorToHttp(error, locale)
   - Return: return NextResponse.json(response, { status })
2. Check if result.isRight():
   - Extract project entity: const project = result.value
   - Convert to DTO: const dto = project.toDTO() (or use ProjectMapper.toDTO if separate)
   - Build success response: const response = successResponse(dto)
   - Return: return NextResponse.json(response, { status: 200 })

Ensure proper typing throughout. Import mapDomainErrorToHttp and envelope helpers. Follow Either pattern conventions from domain layer.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests: (1) Valid existing project returns 200 with data in envelope, (2) Non-existent project returns 404 with error in envelope, (3) Invalid slug format returns 400 with validation error, (4) Response structure matches ApiResponse type, (5) Error responses include correct code/message, (6) Success responses include project DTO

tasks.title: Setup local Supabase development environment with Docker and Supabase CLI
tasks.description: Configure local Supabase instance using Docker and Supabase CLI for development, including initialization, database connection setup, and Prisma integration.
tasks.details:
1. **Install Supabase CLI globally**:
   - Run: `npm install -g supabase`
   - Verify installation: `supabase --version`
   - Ensure Docker is installed and running (Docker Desktop or Docker Engine)

2. **Initialize Supabase in project root**:
   - Navigate to project root: `/home/wallace/Projects/portfolio`
   - Run: `supabase init`
   - This creates `supabase/` directory with:
     - `config.toml`: Supabase configuration file
     - `seed.sql`: Database seed file (optional)
   - Add `supabase/.temp/` to `.gitignore` if not already present
   - Commit `supabase/config.toml` to version control

3. **Start local Supabase services with Docker**:
   - Run: `supabase start`
   - This pulls and starts Docker containers for:
     - PostgreSQL (local database on port 54322)
     - Supabase Studio (web UI on port 54323)
     - PostgREST API (port 54321)
     - GoTrue (auth service)
     - Realtime server
   - Wait for all services to start (may take 2-3 minutes on first run)
   - CLI will output connection credentials including DATABASE_URL and DIRECT_URL

4. **Configure environment variables**:
   - Copy local database URLs from CLI output
   - Update `.env` in project root with local Supabase credentials:
     ```
     DATABASE_URL="postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:54322/postgres?pgbouncer=true&connection_limit=1"
     DIRECT_URL="postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:54322/postgres"
     ```
   - Also update `apps/web/.env` if it exists separately
   - Update `.env.example` with placeholders showing the local format

5. **Run Prisma migration against local database**:
   - Navigate to `packages/infra/` directory
   - Ensure `prisma/schema.prisma` exists (already present with complete schema)
   - Run: `pnpm db:migrate` or `npx prisma migrate dev --name init`
   - This creates the initial migration in `packages/infra/prisma/migrations/`
   - Prisma will:
     - Create all tables (Project, Skill, Experience, Profile, etc.)
     - Create all enums (ProjectStatus, SkillType, EmploymentType, LocationType)
     - Create all indexes and foreign key constraints
     - Generate Prisma Client

6. **Verify database setup**:
   - Run: `pnpm db:studio` from `packages/infra/` or `npx prisma studio`
   - Prisma Studio opens at http://localhost:5555
   - Verify all tables exist:
     - Project, ProjectSkill
     - Skill
     - Experience, ExperienceSkill
     - Profile, ProfileStat, SocialNetwork
   - Check enums are properly created
   - Optionally access Supabase Studio at http://localhost:54323

7. **Document local development workflow**:
   - Create or update `packages/infra/README.md` with:
     - Steps to start local Supabase: `supabase start`
     - Steps to stop: `supabase stop`
     - Steps to reset DB: `supabase db reset`
     - Connection string format for local development
     - Note that local DB data is ephemeral (reset on `supabase stop`)
   - Add npm scripts to root `package.json`:
     ```json
     "db:start": "supabase start",
     "db:stop": "supabase stop",
     "db:reset": "supabase db reset"
     ```

8. **Configure .gitignore**:
   - Ensure `.gitignore` includes:
     ```
     # Supabase
     supabase/.temp/
     supabase/.branches/
     **/supabase/.temp/
     ```
   - DO commit: `supabase/config.toml`, `supabase/migrations/`, `supabase/seed.sql`

**Important Notes**:
- This task sets up LOCAL development only (not production Supabase)
- The schema at `packages/infra/prisma/schema.prisma` already exists with complete models (Project, Experience, Profile, Skill tables)
- Migration name `init` is used because this is the first migration
- Local Supabase runs on different ports (54322 for DB) than production (5432)
- Docker must be running before `supabase start`
- Depends on Prisma schema being defined (Task 1: packages/infra setup)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **Verify CLI installation**:
   - Run: `supabase --version`
   - Should output version number (e.g., `1.x.x`)
   - Run: `docker --version` to confirm Docker is available

2. **Verify Supabase initialization**:
   - Check `supabase/config.toml` exists in project root
   - Verify file contains database configuration sections
   - Confirm `supabase/` directory structure is correct

3. **Verify Docker containers are running**:
   - Run: `docker ps`
   - Should show containers: `supabase-db`, `supabase-studio`, `supabase-rest`, `supabase-auth`, `supabase-realtime`
   - Run: `supabase status` to see all service URLs and credentials

4. **Verify environment variables**:
   - Check `.env` contains valid DATABASE_URL and DIRECT_URL
   - Verify connection strings use `localhost:54322`
   - Test connection string format matches Supabase CLI output

5. **Verify Prisma migration**:
   - Check `packages/infra/prisma/migrations/` directory exists
   - Verify migration folder created (e.g., `20240101000000_init/`)
   - Confirm `migration.sql` contains CREATE TABLE statements for all models
   - Run: `pnpm -C packages/infra db:generate` to verify Prisma Client generation

6. **Verify database schema with Prisma Studio**:
   - Run: `pnpm -C packages/infra db:studio`
   - Access http://localhost:5555
   - Verify all tables visible:
     - Project (with fields: id, slug, title, caption, status, etc.)
     - Skill (with fields: id, description, icon, type)
     - Experience (with fields: id, company, position, employmentType, etc.)
     - Profile (with fields: id, name, headline, bio, photoUrl)
     - ProjectSkill, ExperienceSkill, ProfileStat, SocialNetwork (join/relation tables)
   - Verify enums: ProjectStatus, SkillType, EmploymentType, LocationType

7. **Verify Supabase Studio access**:
   - Access http://localhost:54323
   - Should show Supabase Studio UI
   - Navigate to Table Editor
   - Verify same tables are visible as in Prisma Studio

8. **Test database connection**:
   - Create simple test file to verify Prisma Client can query the database:
     ```typescript
     import { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client'
     const prisma = new PrismaClient()
     await prisma.$connect()
     console.log('Connected to database')
     await prisma.$disconnect()
     ```
   - Run test to confirm successful connection

9. **Verify npm scripts**:
   - Run: `pnpm db:start` (should start Supabase)
   - Run: `pnpm db:stop` (should stop Supabase)
   - Verify commands execute without errors

10. **Verify .gitignore configuration**:
    - Confirm `supabase/.temp/` is ignored
    - Confirm `supabase/config.toml` is NOT ignored (tracked by git)
    - Run: `git status` to verify only intended files are tracked

subtasks.title: Install Supabase CLI and initialize Supabase in project root
subtasks.description: Install Supabase CLI globally, verify installation, initialize Supabase directory structure, and configure .gitignore for Supabase-specific files.

subtasks.title: Start local Supabase services with Docker and extract connection credentials
subtasks.description: Start local Supabase Docker containers, wait for all services to initialize, and extract DATABASE_URL and DIRECT_URL from CLI output for local development.
subtasks.details:
1. Ensure Docker is running (check with `docker ps` or Docker Desktop)
2. Start Supabase services: `supabase start` from project root
3. Wait for Docker to pull and start all containers (2-3 minutes on first run):
   - PostgreSQL (local database on port 54322, not standard 5432)
   - Supabase Studio (web UI on port 54323)
   - PostgREST API (port 54321)
   - GoTrue (auth service)
   - Realtime server
   - Inbucket (email testing)
4. CLI will output connection details including:
   - `DB URL`: postgresql connection string for pooled connection (port 54322)
   - `Direct URL`: postgresql connection string for direct connection (port 54322)
   - `API URL`: http://localhost:54321
   - `Studio URL`: http://localhost:54323
   - `anon key`: for client-side access
   - `service_role key`: for server-side admin access
5. Copy both DATABASE_URL and DIRECT_URL values from CLI output
6. Note: Local Supabase uses different ports (54322) than production Supabase (5432)
7. Keep terminal window open or note that services run in background Docker containers
subtasks.testStrategy:

2. Verify Docker containers are running: `docker ps | grep supabase`
3. Verify Studio is accessible: open http://localhost:54323 in browser
4. Verify PostgreSQL is accessible: `docker exec supabase_db_portfolio psql -U postgres -c 'SELECT version();'` (container name may vary)
5. Verify CLI output contains DATABASE_URL starting with `postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:54322/postgres`

subtasks.title: Run Prisma migration against local database and generate Prisma Client
subtasks.description: Execute initial Prisma migration to create all tables, enums, indexes, and foreign key constraints in the local Supabase database, and generate Prisma Client for TypeScript access.
subtasks.details:
1. Navigate to `packages/infra/` directory
2. Verify `prisma/schema.prisma` exists (already present with complete schema)
3. Run migration: `pnpm db:migrate` (which runs `prisma migrate dev`)
   - This creates the initial migration in `packages/infra/prisma/migrations/`
   - Migration name will be auto-generated or prompted (suggest: `init` or `initial_schema`)
4. Prisma will automatically:
   - Create all tables: Project, Skill, Experience, Profile, ProjectSkill, ExperienceSkill, ProfileStat, SocialNetwork
   - Create all enums: ProjectStatus, SkillType, EmploymentType, LocationType
   - Create all indexes and foreign key constraints as defined in schema
   - Generate Prisma Client in `node_modules/.prisma/client/`
5. If migration fails, check:
   - DATABASE_URL and DIRECT_URL are correctly set in `.env`
   - Local Supabase is running: `supabase status`
   - Connection to localhost:54322 is accessible
6. Migration files will be created in `packages/infra/prisma/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_migration_name/`
7. Commit the generated migration files to git

subtasks.title: Document local development workflow and add npm scripts to root package.json
subtasks.description: Update packages/infra/README.md with comprehensive local Supabase workflow documentation and add convenience scripts to root package.json for starting, stopping, and resetting local database.
subtasks.details:
1. Update `packages/infra/README.md` to replace WIP status with local development section:
   - Add "Local Development" section with:
     - Prerequisites: Docker Desktop or Docker Engine running
     - Start services: `supabase start` (from project root)
     - Stop services: `supabase stop` (from project root)
     - Reset database: `supabase db reset` (drops all data, re-runs migrations and seeds)
     - View data: `pnpm db:studio` (from packages/infra/) or http://localhost:54323 (Supabase Studio)
   - Add connection string format explanation:
     - Local: `postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:54322/postgres`
     - Production: `postgresql://postgres.[project-ref]:[password]@aws-0-[region].pooler.supabase.com:5432/postgres`
   - Add note: Local DB data is ephemeral when using `supabase stop` without `--no-backup` flag
   - Add troubleshooting section:
     - "Services won't start": Check Docker is running, check port conflicts (54321-54323, 54322)
     - "Migration fails": Verify .env has correct DATABASE_URL, run `supabase status` to check services
2. Add npm scripts to root `package.json`:
   ```json
   "db:start": "supabase start",
   "db:stop": "supabase stop",
   "db:reset": "supabase db reset",
   "db:status": "supabase status",
   "db:studio": "pnpm -C packages/infra db:studio"
   ```
3. Update `.gitignore` verification section to confirm:
   - `supabase/.temp/` is ignored (local temp files)
   - `supabase/.branches/` is ignored (local branch data)
   - `supabase/config.toml` is NOT ignored (should be committed)
   - `supabase/migrations/` is NOT ignored (should be committed)
4. Add note in README about Task 1 dependency (Prisma schema must exist first)

5. Test scripts from project root:

   - `pnpm db:start` restarts services successfully

7. Verify documentation is clear and actionable for another developer

tasks.title: Refactor Experience.skills from ExperienceSkill VO to Id[] array
tasks.description: Remove ExperienceSkill value object from core domain layer and replace Experience.skills with a simple Id[] array. Update application DTOs to return string[] for skill IDs. Update infrastructure mapper and repository to work with skill ID references only. Drop workDescription column from Prisma schema.
tasks.details:
This refactoring simplifies the Experience entity by removing the ExperienceSkill VO and workDescription concept, treating skills as simple references via Id[].

**1. Remove ExperienceSkill VO from packages/core**
   - Delete `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/experience/model/ExperienceSkill.ts`
   - Delete `packages/core/test/experience/ExperienceSkill.test.ts`
   - Update `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/experience/index.ts`:
     - Remove `export * from './model/ExperienceSkill';`
   - Update `packages/core/test/helpers/builders/ExperienceBuilder.ts`:
     - Remove ExperienceSkill imports and usages
     - Replace skill props with simple Id[] array

**2. Update Experience entity in packages/core**
   - File: `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/experience/model/Experience.ts`
   - Change interface `IExperienceProps`:
     - Replace `skills: IExperienceSkillProps[]` with `skills: string[]` (UUID strings)
   - Change class property:
     - Replace `public readonly skills: ExperienceSkill[]` with `public readonly skills: Id[]`
   - Update `create()` method:
     - Remove ExperienceSkill.create() loop (lines 99-104)
     - Replace with Id validation loop:
       ```typescript
       const skills: Id[] = [];
       for (const skillId of props.skills ?? []) {
         const idResult = Id.create(skillId);
         if (idResult.isLeft()) return left(idResult.value);
         skills.push(idResult.value);
       }
       ```
   - Update constructor signature to accept `skills: Id[]`
   - Remove ExperienceSkill import, keep Id import from shared

**3. Update Experience entity tests**
   - File: `packages/core/test/experience/Experience.test.ts`
   - Replace ExperienceSkill test cases with Id[] validation tests
   - Test invalid UUID in skills array returns left
   - Test valid UUIDs create Experience successfully

**4. Delete ExperienceSkillDTO from packages/application**
   - Delete `packages/application/src/portfolio/dtos/ExperienceSkillDTO.ts`
   - Update `packages/application/src/portfolio/dtos/index.ts`:
     - Remove `export * from './ExperienceSkillDTO';`

**5. Update ExperienceDTO in packages/application**
   - File: `packages/application/src/portfolio/dtos/ExperienceDTO.ts`
   - Remove ExperienceSkillDTO import
   - Change `skills: ExperienceSkillDTO[]` to `skills: string[]` (plain UUID strings)

**6. Update GetExperiences use case**
   - File: `packages/application/src/portfolio/use-cases/GetExperiences.ts`
   - Remove ExperienceSkillDTO import
   - Remove `toSkillDTO()` method (lines 50-57)
   - Update `toDTO()` method line 46:
     - Replace `skills: experience.skills.map((s) => this.toSkillDTO(s, locale))`
     - With `skills: experience.skills.map((id) => id.value)`

**7. Update ExperienceMapper in packages/infra**
   - File: `packages/infra/src/repositories/experience/ExperienceMapper.ts`
   - Remove IExperienceSkillProps import
   - In `toDomain()` method (lines 41-51):
     - Replace skill mapping logic with simple:
       ```typescript
       const skillIds = raw.skills.map((es) => es.skillId);
       ```
   - Update props construction (line 65):
     - Replace `skills: skillProps` with `skills: skillIds`
   - Remove workDescription handling entirely

**8. Update PrismaExperienceRepository**
   - File: `packages/infra/src/repositories/experience/PrismaExperienceRepository.ts`
   - Update `save()` method (lines 36-39):
     - Remove workDescription from skillsCreate:
       ```typescript
       const skillsCreate = experience.skills.map((skillId) => ({
         skillId: skillId.value,
       }));
       ```

**9. Update ExperienceMapper tests**
   - File: `packages/infra/test/repositories/experience/ExperienceMapper.test.ts`
   - Remove workDescription assertions
   - Update test fixtures to use simple skill ID arrays

**10. Drop workDescription column from Prisma schema**
   - File: `packages/infra/prisma/schema.prisma`
   - Update ExperienceSkill model (lines 127-136):
     - Remove `workDescription Json` field (line 130)
     - Keep only experienceId, skillId, and relations
   - Generate migration:
     ```bash
     cd packages/infra
     pnpm prisma migrate dev --name remove_experience_skill_work_description
     ```
   - Commit the generated migration file

**11. Clean up imports across the codebase**
   - Search for any remaining ExperienceSkill imports and remove them
   - Verify no other files reference ExperienceSkillDTO
   - Update any seed scripts or fixtures if they exist

**Files to modify:**
- packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/experience/model/Experience.ts
- packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/experience/index.ts
- packages/core/test/experience/Experience.test.ts
- packages/core/test/helpers/builders/ExperienceBuilder.ts
- packages/application/src/portfolio/dtos/ExperienceDTO.ts
- packages/application/src/portfolio/dtos/index.ts
- packages/application/src/portfolio/use-cases/GetExperiences.ts
- packages/infra/src/repositories/experience/ExperienceMapper.ts
- packages/infra/src/repositories/experience/PrismaExperienceRepository.ts
- packages/infra/test/repositories/experience/ExperienceMapper.test.ts
- packages/infra/prisma/schema.prisma

**Files to delete:**
- packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/experience/model/ExperienceSkill.ts
- packages/core/test/experience/ExperienceSkill.test.ts
- packages/application/src/portfolio/dtos/ExperienceSkillDTO.ts
tasks.testStrategy:

   - Test Experience.create() with empty skills array succeeds

**4. Unit tests for GetExperiences use case**

tasks.title: Convert SkillType, Fluency, LocationType, EmploymentType from VOs to inline enums
tasks.description: Refactor SkillType, Fluency, LocationType, and EmploymentType from dedicated Value Objects to inline TypeScript enums defined directly within their owning entities (Skill, Language, Experience). Delete the four VO files from packages/core/src/shared/vo/, move type definitions and constants into their respective entities, and validate using Validator.of(value).in([...]).validate() within each entity's create() method.
tasks.details:
This refactoring simplifies the domain model by replacing over-engineered Value Objects for stable enums with inline TypeScript enums and Validator-based validation within entities.

**1. Define inline enums within owning entities**

**Skill entity (packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/skill/model/Skill.ts)**
- Add SkillType enum at top of file:
  ```typescript
  export enum SkillType {
    EDUCATION = 'EDUCATION',
    TECHNOLOGY = 'TECHNOLOGY',
    LANGUAGE = 'LANGUAGE',
    SOFT = 'SOFT',
    OTHER = 'OTHER',
  }
  ```
- Change `ISkillProps.type` from `SkillTypeValue` to `SkillType`
- Change `Skill.type` from `SkillType` VO to `SkillType` enum (remove from class properties that reference the VO)
- Update `Skill.create()`:
  - Remove `SkillType.create(props.type)` from collect()
  - Add Validator check after collect():
    ```typescript
    const { error, isValid } = Validator.of(props.type)
      .in(Object.values(SkillType), 'Invalid skill type.')
      .validate();
    if (!isValid && error)
      return left(new ValidationError({ code: 'INVALID_SKILL_TYPE', message: error }));
    ```
  - Update constructor call to pass `props.type` directly (no VO unwrapping)
- Remove `SkillType` VO import from shared

**Language entity (packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/language/model/Language.ts)**
- Add Fluency enum at top of file:
  ```typescript
  export enum Fluency {
    BEGINNER = 'BEGINNER',
    ELEMENTARY = 'ELEMENTARY',
    INTERMEDIATE = 'INTERMEDIATE',
    UPPER_INTERMEDIATE = 'UPPER-INTERMEDIATE',
    ADVANCED = 'ADVANCED',
    NATIVE = 'NATIVE',
  }
  ```
- Change `ILanguageProps.fluency` from `FluencyValue` to `Fluency`
- Change `Language.fluency` from `Fluency` VO to `Fluency` enum
- Update `Language.create()`:
  - Remove `Fluency.create(props.fluency)` from collect()
  - Add Validator check after collect():
    ```typescript
    const { error, isValid } = Validator.of(props.fluency)
      .in(Object.values(Fluency), 'Invalid fluency level.')
      .validate();
    if (!isValid && error)
      return left(new ValidationError({ code: 'INVALID_FLUENCY', message: error }));
    ```
  - Update constructor call to pass `props.fluency` directly
- Remove `Fluency` VO import from shared

**Experience entity (packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/experience/model/Experience.ts)**
- Add LocationType and EmploymentType enums at top of file:
  ```typescript
  export enum LocationType {
    ON_SITE = 'ON-SITE',
    HYBRID = 'HYBRID',
    REMOTE = 'REMOTE',
  }

  export enum EmploymentType {
    FULL_TIME = 'FULL_TIME',
    PART_TIME = 'PART_TIME',
    SELF_EMPLOYED = 'SELF_EMPLOYED',
    FREELANCE = 'FREELANCE',
    TEMPORARY = 'TEMPORARY',
    INTERNSHIP = 'INTERNSHIP',
    APPRENTICE = 'APPRENTICE',
    TRAINEE = 'TRAINEE',
  }
  ```
- Change `IExperienceProps.employment_type` from `EmploymentTypeValue` to `EmploymentType`
- Change `IExperienceProps.location_type` from `LocationTypeValue` to `LocationType`
- Change `Experience.employment_type` from `EmploymentType` VO to `EmploymentType` enum
- Change `Experience.location_type` from `LocationType` VO to `LocationType` enum
- Update `Experience.create()`:
  - Remove `EmploymentType.create(props.employment_type)` and `LocationType.create(props.location_type)` from collect()
  - Add Validator checks after collect():
    ```typescript
    const employmentResult = Validator.of(props.employment_type)
      .in(Object.values(EmploymentType), 'Invalid employment type.')
      .validate();
    if (!employmentResult.isValid && employmentResult.error)
      return left(new ValidationError({ code: 'INVALID_EMPLOYMENT_TYPE', message: employmentResult.error }));

    const locationResult = Validator.of(props.location_type)
      .in(Object.values(LocationType), 'Invalid location type.')
      .validate();
    if (!locationResult.isValid && locationResult.error)
      return left(new ValidationError({ code: 'INVALID_LOCATION_TYPE', message: locationResult.error }));
    ```
  - Update constructor call to pass `props.employment_type` and `props.location_type` directly
- Remove `EmploymentType`, `LocationType` VO imports from shared

**2. Delete VO files from packages/core/src/shared/vo/**
- Delete `SkillType.ts`
- Delete `Fluency.ts`
- Delete `LocationType.ts`
- Delete `EmploymentType.ts`
- Update `packages/core/src/shared/vo/index.ts`: remove exports for these four VOs
- Update `packages/core/src/shared/index.ts`: ensure no exports reference deleted VOs (they should come from vo/index.ts)

**3. Update all importers to use new enum types**

**Infrastructure mappers**
- `packages/infra/src/repositories/experience/ExperienceMapper.ts`:
  - Replace `LocationTypeValue` import with `LocationType` enum from Experience entity
  - Update LOCATION_TYPE_MAP to map Prisma enum values to `LocationType` enum values
  - Update LOCATION_TYPE_REVERSE_MAP accordingly
  - In `toDomain()`: use `EmploymentType` and `LocationType` enums directly (no .value unwrapping)
  - In `toPrisma()`: access `experience.employment_type` and `experience.location_type` directly (no .value)

- `packages/infra/src/repositories/project/ProjectMapper.ts`:
  - Import `SkillType` enum from Skill entity (if referenced)
  - Update any SkillType VO unwrapping to direct enum access

- `packages/infra/src/repositories/profile/ProfileMapper.ts`:
  - Check for any Language/Fluency references and update imports

**Application DTOs**
- `packages/application/src/portfolio/dtos/ExperienceDTO.ts`:
  - Update `employmentType` and `locationType` field types if needed (already strings, should remain compatible)
  
- Review all DTOs for Skill, Language references:
  - Ensure DTO serialization uses enum values directly (e.g., `skill.type` instead of `skill.type.value`)

**4. Update all tests**

**Delete VO unit tests**
- Delete `packages/core/test/shared/vo/SkillType.test.ts`
- Delete `packages/core/test/shared/vo/Fluency.test.ts`
- Delete `packages/core/test/shared/vo/LocationType.test.ts`
- Delete `packages/core/test/shared/vo/EmploymentType.test.ts`

**Update entity tests**
- `packages/core/test/skill/Skill.test.ts`:
  - Import `SkillType` enum from Skill entity
  - Update test factories to pass enum values directly (not VO instances)
  - Add test: "should return left when type is invalid" with invalid enum value
  
- `packages/core/test/language/Language.test.ts`:
  - Import `Fluency` enum from Language entity
  - Update test factories to pass enum values directly
  - Add test: "should return left when fluency is invalid"
  
- `packages/core/test/experience/Experience.test.ts`:
  - Import `LocationType` and `EmploymentType` enums from Experience entity
  - Update test factories to pass enum values directly
  - Add tests: "should return left when employment_type is invalid" and "should return left when location_type is invalid"

**Update test helpers/generators**
- `packages/core/test/helpers/generators/`:
  - Update `SkillTypeData.ts`, `FluencyData.ts`, `LocationTypeData.ts`, `EmploymentTypeData.ts` to export raw enum values instead of VO factory functions
  - Or delete these files if they only existed to generate VO instances
  - Update `index.ts` to export enum values directly

**Update infra repository tests**
- `packages/infra/test/repositories/experience/PrismaExperienceRepository.test.ts`:
  - Import enums from entities instead of VOs
  - Update mock data to use enum values directly
  
- `packages/infra/test/repositories/project/PrismaProjectRepository.test.ts`:
  - Import `SkillType` from Skill entity
  - Update mock data

**5. Update entity index exports**
- `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/skill/index.ts`: export SkillType enum
- `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/language/index.ts`: export Fluency enum
- `packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/experience/index.ts`: export LocationType and EmploymentType enums

**6. Verify no remaining references**
- Search codebase for imports from `'@repo/core/shared'` that reference deleted VOs
- Search for `.value` access on `type`, `fluency`, `employment_type`, `location_type` properties (should be removed)
- Ensure no remaining files import `SkillTypeValue`, `FluencyValue`, `LocationTypeValue`, `EmploymentTypeValue` types
tasks.testStrategy:

tasks.title: Create AggregateRoot abstract class and refactor entities
tasks.description: Introduce AggregateRoot<T, TProps> abstract class extending Entity in packages/core/src/shared/base/AggregateRoot.ts with empty body. Refactor Experience, Skill, Project, and Profile to extend AggregateRoot. Evaluate Language, ProfessionalValue, SocialNetwork, and ProfileStat to assign them as aggregate roots with repositories or as internal entities/VOs inside Profile.
tasks.details:
This task establishes the DDD aggregate root pattern in the domain model, distinguishing aggregate roots (entities with repositories and transactional boundaries) from internal entities and value objects.

**1. Create AggregateRoot.ts base class**

Create `packages/core/src/shared/base/AggregateRoot.ts`:

```typescript
import { Entity, IEntityProps } from './Entity';

/**
 * AggregateRoot abstract class.
 * Marks entities that serve as transactional consistency boundaries
 * and have their own repositories for persistence.
 */
export abstract class AggregateRoot<
  T,
  TProps extends IEntityProps
> extends Entity<T, TProps> {}
```

**2. Export AggregateRoot from shared/base/index.ts**

Update `packages/core/src/shared/base/index.ts`:
```typescript
export * from './Entity';
export * from './ValueObject';
export * from './AggregateRoot';
```

**3. Refactor confirmed aggregate roots to extend AggregateRoot**

Based on existing repository interfaces (IProjectRepository, IExperienceRepository, IProfileRepository, ISkillRepository) and DDD documentation in `docs/03-BOUNDED-CONTEXTS.md`, these entities have repositories and should extend AggregateRoot:

**Project (packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/project/model/Project.ts:49)**
- Change `extends Entity<Project, IProjectProps>` to `extends AggregateRoot<Project, IProjectProps>`
- Import AggregateRoot from shared/base
- Justification: Has IProjectRepository with findById, findBySlug, save, delete operations

**Experience (packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/experience/model/Experience.ts:38)**
- Change `extends Entity<Experience, IExperienceProps>` to `extends AggregateRoot<Experience, IExperienceProps>`
- Import AggregateRoot from shared/base
- Justification: Has IExperienceRepository (confirmed in grep results)

**Profile (packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/profile/model/Profile.ts:30)**
- Change `extends Entity<Profile, IProfileProps>` to `extends AggregateRoot<Profile, IProfileProps>`
- Import AggregateRoot from shared/base
- Justification: Has IProfileRepository (singleton pattern with find/save)

**Skill (packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/skill/model/Skill.ts:20)**
- Change `extends Entity<Skill, ISkillProps>` to `extends AggregateRoot<Skill, ISkillProps>`
- Import AggregateRoot from shared/base
- Justification: Has ISkillRepository (confirmed in grep results)

**4. Evaluate and classify remaining entities**

**Language** (packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/language/model/Language.ts:25)
- Decision: Keep as Entity (NOT aggregate root)
- Rationale: Language appears to be a reference entity shared across contexts (e.g., Profile languages). Based on DDD patterns, if Language is managed globally and referenced by Profile without Profile owning its lifecycle, it should remain an independent entity with potential repository but NOT an aggregate root in the Portfolio context. However, review existing repository structure first.
- Action: Inspect for ILanguageRepository. If none exists and Language is always managed within Profile, consider making it a VO or internal entity.

**ProfessionalValue** (packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/professional-value/model/ProfessionalValue.ts:17)
- Decision: Keep as Entity (NOT aggregate root)
- Rationale: Professional values appear to be a collection managed by Profile. No independent repository interface found. Should be internal entities within Profile aggregate.
- Action: Keep extending Entity. Profile is responsible for managing ProfessionalValue lifecycle.

**SocialNetwork** (packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/social-network/model/SocialNetwork.ts:20)
- Decision: Keep as Entity (NOT aggregate root)
- Rationale: SocialNetwork is a child entity within Profile aggregate (confirmed in Profile.ts and PrismaProfileRepository implementation). Profile owns SocialNetwork lifecycle.
- Action: Keep extending Entity. Managed via Profile repository.

**ProfileStat** (packages/core/src/portfolio/entities/profile/model/ProfileStat.ts:20)
- Decision: Keep as plain class (NOT entity, NOT aggregate root)
- Rationale: ProfileStat does not extend Entity currently. It's a simple value object with no identity (no id field in IProfileStatProps). It's always managed within Profile.
- Action: Leave as-is (plain class). ProfileStat is effectively a value object within Profile aggregate.

**5. Update entity index exports if needed**

Verify each entity's index.ts exports the refactored class correctly. No changes should be needed beyond the class declaration updates.

**6. Update test builders**

Update test builders in `packages/core/test/helpers/builders/` for Project, Experience, Profile, and Skill:
- ProjectBuilder.ts
- ExperienceBuilder.ts
- ProfileBuilder.ts
- SkillBuilder.ts

Verify builders still work correctly after entities extend AggregateRoot instead of Entity (should be transparent since AggregateRoot extends Entity).

**Architecture Decision Record:**

Aggregate Roots (extend AggregateRoot, have repositories):
- Project ✓
- Experience ✓
- Profile ✓
- Skill ✓

Internal Entities (extend Entity, managed by aggregates):
- Language (verify if has repository; if not, internal to Profile)
- ProfessionalValue (internal to Profile)
- SocialNetwork (internal to Profile)

Value Objects (no identity, no Entity base):
- ProfileStat ✓
- ExperienceSkill (VO composition of Skill reference + workDescription)

tasks.title: Implement Identity bounded context core layer
tasks.description: Create the Identity bounded context in packages/core with Email value object, Role enum, User entity, UnauthorizedError domain error, IUserRepository interface, and AccessPolicy utility. Export all from packages/core/src/identity/index.ts and write comprehensive unit tests.
tasks.testStrategy:
**1. Unit tests for Email value object**
- Test `Email.create()` with valid email returns right(Email)
- Test email normalization (trim, lowercase)
- Test invalid emails return left(ValidationError) with code 'INVALID_EMAIL'
- Test length constraints (3-254 characters)
- Test email format regex validation
- Test `domain` and `localPart` getters
- Test equals() comparison

**2. Unit tests for User entity**
- Test `User.create()` with valid props (name, email, ADMIN role) returns right(User)
- Test `User.create()` with VISITOR role succeeds
- Test invalid email returns left(ValidationError)
- Test invalid name returns left(ValidationError)
- Test invalid role (not ADMIN | VISITOR) returns left(ValidationError) with message containing "Role must be one of"
- Test `user.isAdmin()` returns true for ADMIN role, false for VISITOR
- Test `user.isVisitor()` returns true for VISITOR role, false for ADMIN
- Test entity has Name VO and Email VO properties
- Test entity extends Entity base class (id, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at)

**3. Unit tests for AccessPolicy**
- Test `AccessPolicy.isAdmin(user)` returns true for admin user, false for visitor
- Test `AccessPolicy.isVisitor(user)` returns true for visitor user, false for admin
- Test `AccessPolicy.hasRole(user, Role.ADMIN)` returns correct boolean
- Test `AccessPolicy.hasRole(user, Role.VISITOR)` returns correct boolean

**4. Unit tests for UnauthorizedError**
- Test error has code 'UNAUTHORIZED'
- Test error has default message 'Unauthorized access'
- Test error accepts custom message
- Test error extends DomainError
- Test error name is 'UnauthorizedError'

**5. TypeScript compilation**
- Run `pnpm --filter @repo/core types` to verify no type errors
- Verify all exports resolve correctly from `@repo/core/identity`

**6. Linting**
- Run `pnpm --filter @repo/core lint:check` to verify code style

**7. Integration verification**
- Import from `@repo/core/identity` succeeds
- Import from `@repo/core` namespace (identity.User, identity.Email, etc.) succeeds
- Verify IUserRepository interface compiles with Id and Email VOs as parameters

tasks.title: Implement Identity infrastructure in packages/infra
tasks.description: Create Prisma migration for users table, implement SupabaseUserRepository (now PrismaUserRepository) following existing repository patterns, create seed script for initial admin user, and wire the repository into the dependency injection container.
tasks.testStrategy:
**1. Migration verification**
- Run `pnpm --filter @repo/infra db:migrate` and verify migration applies successfully
- Run `pnpm --filter @repo/infra db:studio` and verify `users` table exists with correct schema
- Verify Role enum has ADMIN and VISITOR values
- Verify email has unique constraint

**2. Unit tests for UserMapper**

Create `packages/infra/test/repositories/user/UserMapper.test.ts`:
- Test `toDomain()` converts Prisma user to domain User entity
- Test `toDomain()` throws InfrastructureError on invalid data
- Test `toPrisma()` converts User entity to Prisma input
- Test email normalization and role mapping

**3. Integration tests for PrismaUserRepository**

Create `packages/infra/test/repositories/user/PrismaUserRepository.test.ts`:
- Test `findById()` returns user when exists
- Test `findById()` returns null when not found
- Test `findByEmail()` returns user when exists
- Test `findByEmail()` returns null when not found
- Test `save()` creates new user
- Test `save()` updates existing user (upsert behavior)
- Test email uniqueness constraint

**4. Seed script validation**
- Run `pnpm --filter @repo/infra db:seed` and verify admin user is created
- Verify admin user has role ADMIN
- Run seed script twice and verify idempotency (upsert should not fail)
- Check admin email from env or default value

**5. Container integration test**

Update `packages/infra/test/container.test.ts`:
- Verify `makeContainer()` creates userRepository instance
- Verify userRepository is instanceof PrismaUserRepository
- Test userRepository can execute queries

**6. End-to-end validation**
- Create admin user via seed
- Use container to get userRepository
- Call `findByEmail()` with admin email
- Verify User entity is returned with ADMIN role

tasks.title: Implement Identity application use cases
tasks.description: Create GetCurrentUserUseCase and EnsureAdminUseCase in packages/application/src/identity following the established UseCase pattern. Create UserDTO for data transfer. Export all from packages/application/src/identity/index.ts with comprehensive unit tests using mocked IUserRepository.
tasks.testStrategy:
**1. Unit test execution**
- Run `pnpm --filter @repo/application test identity` to execute all identity use case tests
- Verify GetCurrentUser.test.ts passes all tests (6 scenarios)
- Verify EnsureAdmin.test.ts passes all tests (8 scenarios)

**2. TypeScript compilation verification**
- Run `pnpm --filter @repo/application build` to ensure no type errors
- Verify UserDTO type is correctly exported and accessible
- Verify use cases correctly implement `UseCase<TInput, TOutput, TError>` abstract class
- Verify Either types are correctly composed (UnauthorizedError | NotFoundError | DomainError)

**3. Import/export chain verification**
- In a test file or Node REPL, verify: `import { GetCurrentUser, EnsureAdmin, UserDTO } from '@repo/application/identity'`
- Verify top-level export: `import { GetCurrentUser, EnsureAdmin, UserDTO } from '@repo/application'`

**4. Mock repository verification**
- Verify tests use mocked `IUserRepository` from `@repo/core/identity`
- Verify mocks follow the pattern from GetProfile.test.ts (makeRepository helper)
- Verify all IUserRepository methods are mocked (findById, findByEmail, save)

**5. Error handling coverage**
- Verify GetCurrentUser returns NotFoundError when user not found
- Verify GetCurrentUser returns DomainError on repository exception
- Verify EnsureAdmin returns UnauthorizedError for non-admin users
- Verify EnsureAdmin returns NotFoundError when user not found
- Verify EnsureAdmin returns DomainError on repository exception

**6. DTO mapping verification**
- Verify UserDTO correctly maps id, email, and role from User entity
- Verify UserDTO uses primitive strings (not VOs) for all fields
- Verify role is returned as-is (not transformed)

**7. AccessPolicy integration**
- Verify EnsureAdmin uses `AccessPolicy.isAdmin(user)` for authorization check
- Verify behavior differs between ADMIN and VISITOR roles
- Verify admin users pass authorization, visitor users fail with UnauthorizedError

**8. Behavior verification (not implementation)**
- Tests verify what the use cases return, not how they implement it
- Tests verify correct error types, not internal implementation details
- Tests verify DTO structure matches specification

**9. Integration with existing tests**
- Run full application test suite: `pnpm --filter @repo/application test`
- Verify no regressions in portfolio or contact use cases
- Verify total test count increases appropriately (14 new tests)

tasks.title: Implement Identity web layer in apps/web
tasks.description: Create getAuthenticatedUser() server helper from Supabase session. Update middleware.ts to protect /[locale]/admin/* and redirect to /[locale]/login if unauthenticated. Create login page at /[locale]/login with Supabase Auth form. Create /[locale]/admin/layout.tsx calling EnsureAdminUseCase and redirecting non-admins.
tasks.testStrategy:
**1. Supabase client creation tests**
- Unit test `createClient()` from `lib/supabase/client.ts` returns valid client
- Verify environment variables are read correctly
- Mock environment variables and verify client configuration

**2. getAuthenticatedUser() helper tests**
- Unit test with mocked Supabase client and userRepository
- Test returns `right(null)` when no session exists
- Test returns `right(User)` when session exists and user found in database
- Test returns `right(null)` when session exists but user not found
- Test returns `left(DomainError)` when email is invalid
- Test returns `left(DomainError)` when database lookup fails

**3. Middleware integration tests**
- Test unauthenticated request to `/en-US/admin` redirects to `/en-US/login?redirect=/en-US/admin`
- Test authenticated request to `/en-US/admin` passes through
- Test non-admin routes (like `/en-US`, `/en-US/projects`) are not affected
- Test i18n routing still works correctly after auth middleware integration
- Test all three locales (en-US, es, pt-BR) work correctly with admin routes

**4. Login page tests**
- Component test: form renders with email and password inputs
- Component test: form submission calls Supabase `signInWithPassword`
- Component test: error message displays when login fails
- Component test: loading state displays during submission
- Component test: successful login redirects to admin or specified redirect param
- Component test: redirect parameter is respected (e.g., `?redirect=/admin/projects`)

**5. Admin layout integration tests**
- Test unauthenticated user redirects to login
- Test authenticated non-admin user redirects to home with error
- Test authenticated admin user sees admin layout and children
- Test EnsureAdminUseCase is called with correct user ID
- Test layout displays admin navigation

**6. End-to-end flow tests**
- Manual test: Navigate to `/en-US/admin` while logged out → redirects to `/en-US/login`
- Manual test: Login with valid admin credentials → redirects to `/en-US/admin`
- Manual test: Login with valid non-admin credentials → redirects to home with error
- Manual test: Access `/en-US/admin` while logged in as admin → shows admin page
- Manual test: Logout and verify session cleared

**7. Container integration test**
- Verify `getContainer()` includes `userRepository`
- Verify `userRepository` is instance of `PrismaUserRepository`
- Integration test: container resolves all dependencies including userRepository

**8. Build and type-checking verification**
- Run `pnpm --filter web build` and verify no TypeScript errors
- Run `pnpm --filter web types` and verify all types compile
- Verify no ESLint errors in new auth files
- Verify middleware config matcher includes all necessary routes
